Looking at the screenshot, I can see:
1. "Test Of In Line Headers" - orange text, no nesting (correct - this is an inline header)
2. "Test of new section" - bold white text, no nesting (this should probably be nested/collapsible)
3. "Test Of Inline Header" - bold white text, no nesting (correct - this is an inline header)

The issue is that I changed the logic to `bool shouldBeCollapsible = detail.collapsible;` which means ONLY sections with `collapsible: true` explicitly set will be collapsible.

But the user wants:
- Sections with `collapsible: true` → nested/collapsible (dropdown)
- Sections with `collapsible: false` (or not set, defaulting to false) AND have a title → inline header (no dropdown, just styled title + flat text)
- The default behavior for sections WITH a title should still be collapsible

So the correct logic should be:
- If `collapsible` is explicitly set to `false`, render as inline
- If `collapsible` is `true` OR if it's not explicitly set (and has a title), render as collapsible

The problem is that `collapsible` defaults to `false` in the model. So I need a way to distinguish between "explicitly set to false" and "not set (defaulting to false)".

Looking at the OperationalDetail model:
```csharp
public bool collapsible { get; set; } = false;
```

This defaults to false. So when JSON doesn't specify it, it's false. When JSON specifies `"collapsible": false`, it's also false. There's no way to distinguish.

The user's intent seems to be:
- By default, sections with titles should be collapsible (nested with dropdown)
- Only when `collapsible: false` is explicitly set should it be an inline header

But with the current model, we can't tell the difference between "not specified" and "explicitly false".


I need to either change the default behavior back to making sections with titles collapsible, or introduce a new property like `inlineHeader: true` or make `collapsible` nullable to distinguish between unset and explicitly false. The old code made everything with a title collapsible regardless of the `collapsible` setting, while the new code only respects the explicit true value.

Looking at what the user actually needs: sections should be collapsible by default when they have a title, but `collapsible: false` should force an inline header instead. The challenge is distinguishing between "not specified" and "explicitly set to false." The cleanest approach is to set `collapsible` to default to `true` in the model itself, so unspecified values automatically become collapsible, explicit true stays collapsible, and explicit false renders inline. I should update the model's default value.I see the issue. The default behavior should be that sections with titles ARE collapsible (nested), and only when `collapsible: false` is explicitly set should it render as an inline header. The problem is that `collapsible` defaults to `false`, so everything became inline.

Let me fix this by changing the default value to `true`: Done. Changed the default value of `collapsible` from `false` to `true`.

Now the behavior is:
- **Default (not specified)**: `collapsible: true` → Nested with dropdown
- **`collapsible: true`**: Nested with dropdown  
- **`collapsible: false`**: Inline header (just title + flat text, no dropdown)

```json
  "relativeWorkspacePath": "c:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaEditor\\src\\renderer\\editor\\ImagePickerModal.tsx"
}
```
Additional data:

```json
{
  "vscodeToolId": "copilot_createFile",
  "isHidden": false
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaEditor/src/renderer/editor/ImagePickerModal.tsx)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaEditor/src/renderer/editor/ImagePickerModal.tsx)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "/**\n * ImagePickerModal - Modal to browse and select PNG images from extracted assets\n */\nimport React, { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react';\n\ninterface ImageInfo {\n  filename: string;\n  path: string;\n}\n\ninterface ImagePickerModalProps {\n  isOpen: boolean;\n  onClose: () =&gt; void;\n  onSelectImage: (filename: string) =&gt; void;\n  currentValue?: string;\n}\n\nexport const ImagePickerModal: React.FC&lt;ImagePickerModalProps&gt; = ({\n  isOpen,\n  onClose,\n  onSelectImage,\n  currentValue,\n}) =&gt; {\n  const [images, setImages] = useState&lt;ImageInfo[]&gt;([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [error, setError] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n  const [searchFilter, setSearchFilter] = useState('');\n  const [selectedImage, setSelectedImage] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(currentValue || null);\n  const [previewImage, setPreviewImage] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n\n  // Load images when modal opens\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    if (isOpen) {\n      loadImages();\n      setSelectedImage(currentValue || null);\n    }\n  }, [isOpen, currentValue]);\n\n  const loadImages = async () =&gt; {\n    setLoading(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      // Use the Electron API to scan the Texture2D folder\n      const result = await window.electronAPI?.scanImageFolder?.();\n      if (result?.images) {\n        setImages(result.images);\n      } else {\n        // Fallback: try to load from a known location\n        setError('Could not load images. Make sure the AssetRipperFiles folder exists.');\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      setError(&#96;Failed to load images: ${err}&#96;);\n    } finally {\n      setLoading(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Filter images based on search\n  const filteredImages = useMemo(() =&gt; {\n    if (!searchFilter.trim()) return images;\n    const filter = searchFilter.toLowerCase();\n    return images.filter(img =&gt; img.filename.toLowerCase().includes(filter));\n  }, [images, searchFilter]);\n\n  // Handle image selection\n  const handleSelect = () =&gt; {\n    if (selectedImage) {\n      onSelectImage(selectedImage);\n      onClose();\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Handle double-click to select immediately\n  const handleDoubleClick = (filename: string) =&gt; {\n    onSelectImage(filename);\n    onClose();\n  };\n\n  if (!isOpen) return null;\n\n  return (\n    &lt;div className=\"fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/70\"&gt;\n      &lt;div className=\"bg-stationpedia-panel border border-stationpedia-border rounded-lg shadow-xl w-[900px] max-h-[80vh] flex flex-col\"&gt;\n        {/* Header */}\n        &lt;div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-stationpedia-border\"&gt;\n          &lt;h2 className=\"text-lg font-semibold text-stationpedia-accent\"&gt;🖼️ Select Page Image&lt;/h2&gt;\n          &lt;button\n            onClick={onClose}\n            className=\"text-gray-400 hover:text-white text-xl\"\n          &gt;\n            ✕\n          &lt;/button&gt;\n        &lt;/div&gt;\n\n        {/* Search Bar */}\n        &lt;div className=\"p-4 border-b border-stationpedia-border\"&gt;\n          &lt;input\n            type=\"text\"\n            value={searchFilter}\n            onChange={(e) =&gt; setSearchFilter(e.target.value)}\n            placeholder=\"Search images by name...\"\n            className=\"w-full px-3 py-2 bg-stationpedia-surface border border-stationpedia-border rounded text-white placeholder-gray-500 focus:outline-none focus:border-stationpedia-accent\"\n            autoFocus\n          /&gt;\n          &lt;div className=\"mt-2 text-xs text-gray-400\"&gt;\n            {filteredImages.length} images found {searchFilter &amp;&amp; &#96;(filtered from ${images.length})&#96;}\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n        &lt;/div&gt;\n\n        {/* Content */}\n        &lt;div className=\"flex-1 overflow-hidden flex\"&gt;\n          {/* Image Grid */}\n          &lt;div className=\"flex-1 overflow-auto p-4\"&gt;\n            {loading ? (\n              &lt;div className=\"flex items-center justify-center h-full text-gray-400\"&gt;\n                Loading images...\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            ) : error ? (\n              &lt;div className=\"flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full text-red-400\"&gt;\n                &lt;div className=\"mb-2\"&gt;⚠️ {error}&lt;/div&gt;\n                &lt;button\n                  onClick={loadImages}\n                  className=\"px-3 py-1 bg-stationpedia-accent hover:bg-stationpedia-accent-hover text-white rounded text-sm\"\n                &gt;\n                  Retry\n                &lt;/button&gt;\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            ) : filteredImages.length === 0 ? (\n              &lt;div className=\"flex items-center justify-center h-full text-gray-400\"&gt;\n                No images found matching \"{searchFilter}\"\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            ) : (\n              &lt;div className=\"grid grid-cols-4 gap-3\"&gt;\n                {filteredImages.map((img) =&gt; (\n                  &lt;div\n                    key={img.filename}\n                    onClick={() =&gt; setSelectedImage(img.filename)}\n                    onDoubleClick={() =&gt; handleDoubleClick(img.filename)}\n                    onMouseEnter={() =&gt; setPreviewImage(img.path)}\n                    onMouseLeave={() =&gt; setPreviewImage(null)}\n                    className={&#96;relative p-2 rounded cursor-pointer border-2 transition-all ${\n                      selectedImage === img.filename\n                        ? 'border-stationpedia-accent bg-stationpedia-accent/20'\n                        : 'border-transparent bg-stationpedia-surface hover:border-stationpedia-border'\n                    }&#96;}\n                    title={img.filename}\n                  &gt;\n                    {/* Thumbnail placeholder - actual image loading would need file:// protocol */}\n                    &lt;div className=\"aspect-square bg-gray-800 rounded flex items-center justify-center overflow-hidden\"&gt;\n                      &lt;img\n                        src={&#96;file://${img.path}&#96;}\n                        alt={img.filename}\n                        className=\"max-w-full max-h-full object-contain\"\n                        onError={(e) =&gt; {\n                          // Replace with placeholder on error\n                          (e.target as HTMLImageElement).style.display = 'none';\n                        }}\n                      /&gt;\n                      &lt;span className=\"absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center text-3xl opacity-30\"&gt;\n                        🖼️\n                      &lt;/span&gt;\n                    &lt;/div&gt;\n                    {/* Filename */}\n                    &lt;div className=\"mt-1 text-xs text-gray-300 truncate text-center\"&gt;\n                      {img.filename.replace('.png', '')}\n                    &lt;/div&gt;\n                  &lt;/div&gt;\n                ))}\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            )}\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n\n          {/* Preview Panel */}\n          &lt;div className=\"w-64 border-l border-stationpedia-border p-4 flex flex-col\"&gt;\n            &lt;div className=\"text-sm font-medium text-gray-300 mb-2\"&gt;Preview&lt;/div&gt;\n            &lt;div className=\"flex-1 bg-gray-800 rounded flex items-center justify-center overflow-hidden\"&gt;\n              {previewImage || selectedImage ? (\n                &lt;img\n                  src={&#96;file://${previewImage || images.find(i =&gt; i.filename === selectedImage)?.path || ''}&#96;}\n                  alt=\"Preview\"\n                  className=\"max-w-full max-h-full object-contain\"\n                  onError={(e) =&gt; {\n                    (e.target as HTMLImageElement).style.display = 'none';\n                  }}\n                /&gt;\n              ) : (\n                &lt;span className=\"text-gray-500 text-sm\"&gt;Hover over an image to preview&lt;/span&gt;\n              )}\n            &lt;/div&gt;\n            {selectedImage &amp;&amp; (\n              &lt;div className=\"mt-2 text-xs text-gray-400 break-all\"&gt;\n                Selected: {selectedImage}\n              &lt;/div&gt;\n            )}\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n        &lt;/div&gt;\n\n        {/* Footer */}\n        &lt;div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-t border-stationpedia-border\"&gt;\n          &lt;div className=\"text-xs text-gray-400\"&gt;\n            Double-click to select, or select and click \"Use Selected\"\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n          &lt;div className=\"flex gap-2\"&gt;\n            &lt;button\n              onClick={onClose}\n              className=\"px-4 py-2 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 text-white rounded\"\n            &gt;\n              Cancel\n            &lt;/button&gt;\n            &lt;button\n              onClick={handleSelect}\n              disabled={!selectedImage}\n              className=\"px-4 py-2 bg-stationpedia-accent hover:bg-stationpedia-accent-hover text-white rounded disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed\"\n            &gt;\n              Use Selected\n            &lt;/button&gt;\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n        &lt;/div&gt;\n      &lt;/div&gt;\n    &lt;/div&gt;\n  );\n};\n\nexport default ImagePickerModal;\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n\r\n\r\n// Scan image folder for PNG files\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:scanImageFolder', async (_, baseFolder?: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Default to the AssetRipperFiles Texture2D folder relative to workspace\r\n    // The workspace path should be passed, or we scan a known location\r\n    const possiblePaths = [\r\n      // Relative to workspace (passed in)\r\n      baseFolder ? path.join(baseFolder, '..', 'AssetRipperFiles', 'ExportedProject', 'Assets', 'Texture2D') : null,\r\n      // Common dev path\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\AssetRipperFiles\\\\ExportedProject\\\\Assets\\\\Texture2D',\r\n    ].filter(Boolean) as string[];\r\n\r\n    let texturePath: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const p of possiblePaths) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(p)) {\r\n        texturePath = p;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!texturePath) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find Texture2D folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const images: { filename: string; path: string }[] = [];\r\n    const entries = fs.readdirSync(texturePath, { withFileTypes: true });\r\n    \r\n    for (const entry of entries) {\r\n      if (entry.isFile() &amp;&amp; entry.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.png')) {\r\n        images.push({\r\n          filename: entry.name,\r\n          path: path.join(texturePath, entry.name),\r\n        });\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Sort alphabetically\r\n    images.sort((a, b) =&gt; a.filename.localeCompare(b.filename));\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, images, folder: texturePath };\r\n  } catch (error: any) {\r\n    return { success: false, error: error.message };\r\n  }\r\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "\r\n\r\n// Scan image folder for PNG files\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:scanImageFolder', async (_, baseFolder?: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Default to the AssetRipperFiles Texture2D folder relative to workspace\r\n    // The workspace path should be passed, or we scan a known location\r\n    const possiblePaths = [\r\n      // Relative to workspace (passed in)\r\n      baseFolder ? path.join(baseFolder, '..', 'AssetRipperFiles', 'ExportedProject', 'Assets', 'Texture2D') : null,\r\n      // Common dev path\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\AssetRipperFiles\\\\ExportedProject\\\\Assets\\\\Texture2D',\r\n    ].filter(Boolean) as string[];\r\n\r\n    let texturePath: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const p of possiblePaths) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(p)) {\r\n        texturePath = p;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!texturePath) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find Texture2D folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const images: { filename: string; path: string }[] = [];\r\n    const entries = fs.readdirSync(texturePath, { withFileTypes: true });\r\n    \r\n    for (const entry of entries) {\r\n      if (entry.isFile() &amp;&amp; entry.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.png')) {\r\n        images.push({\r\n          filename: entry.name,\r\n          path: path.join(texturePath, entry.name),\r\n        });\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Sort alphabetically\r\n    images.sort((a, b) =&gt; a.filename.localeCompare(b.filename));\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, images, folder: texturePath };\r\n  } catch (error: any) {\r\n    return { success: false, error: error.message };\r\n  }\r\n});",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

---

The user wants to add a feature to the editor where they can:
1. Add a page image to a guide
2. When selecting the image, show a popup with all extracted PNG files from the devices so they can choose one

I need to:
1. Check what the editor looks like for guides
2. Find where the device images are stored
3. Create a modal/popup that shows all available PNG images
4. Add a pageImage field editor to the guide editor

Let me start by exploring the editor code structure and finding where guide editing happens.Let me explore the editor structure to understand how to add this feature. Let me look at the DeviceSectionsEditor and the models to understand how guides are edited: Now let me look at how image files are stored/extracted. Let me check the asset browser and how the mod stores images: Now let me check where extracted device images are stored: There are 925 PNG files. Let me also check if there's a Sprite folder which might have more relevant images: The PNG files are in the Texture2D folder. Now let me look at the DeviceSectionsEditor to understand the guide editing UI and add the page image selection: Now I understand the structure. I need to:
1. Add a `pageImage` field to the Guide Settings section in the editor
2. Create an ImagePickerModal component that shows all available PNG files from the extracted assets folder

Let me create the ImagePickerModal and then update the DeviceSectionsEditor: Now I need to add the Electron API to scan images. Let me check the preload and main process: I can see there's already a `listFiles` API that I can use to scan PNG files. Let me add a dedicated handler for scanning images and update the preload. Let me add the handler to scan images: Now update the preload to expose this API: Now let me update the DeviceSectionsEditor to add the pageImage field and the image picker modal. First, let me find the right place to add the page image field for guides: Now I need to add the state for the image picker modal. Let me find the state declarations: Now let me find where the Guide Sections are rendered and add a Page Image section. Let me find the Guide Sections area: I need to add a "Guide Settings" section BEFORE the "Guide Sections" part. Let me find the right place: Now I'll add the Page Image section right before the Guide Sections for JSON guides: Now I need to add the ImagePickerModal at the end of the component (near where LinkModal is): Now I need to add `pageImage` to the expanded sections default state: Now let me update the ImagePickerModal to use the correct Electron API: Now let me also update the image display to use the local-asset protocol instead of file://: Now let's build the mod to make sure the C# changes still work: Mod built successfully. Now let me check for any TypeScript errors in the editor: All files have no errors. Let me provide a summary of what was added:

## Summary of Changes

### C# Mod Changes (already done in previous steps)
- Added `pageImage` field to `GuideDescription` and `DeviceDescriptions` models
- Added `pageDescriptionPrepend` and `pageDescriptionAppend` to `GuideDescription`
- Updated `ToDeviceDescriptions` conversion to include these new fields
- Added `CreateGuideTopImage()` function to render the guide header image
- Updated `RenderGuideSections()` to display page image and description at the top

### Editor Changes

**New File: `ImagePickerModal.tsx`**
- Modal component that displays all 925 PNG files from the extracted assets folder
- Search/filter by filename
- Grid view with thumbnails
- Preview panel on the side
- Double-click or select + "Use Selected" to choose an image

**Updated: `DeviceSectionsEditor.tsx`**
- Added import for `ImagePickerModal`
- Added `isImagePickerOpen` state
- Added "🖼️ Page Image" section for JSON guides with:
  - Text input for image filename
  - "📂 Browse" button to open the image picker
  - Clear button to remove the image
  - Display of current image filename

**Updated: `index.ts` (main process)**
- Added `fs:scanImageFolder` IPC handler that scans the Texture2D folder for PNG files

**Updated: `index.ts` (preload)**
- Exposed `scanImageFolder` API to renderer process
- Updated TypeScript interface

```json
    "scheme": "vscode-terminal",
    "query": "command=tool-8f34de01-e4dc-4231-8004-ca67eeeae953"
  },
  "terminalCommandOutput": {
    "text": "\r\n&gt; descriptions.json:35170:  \"guides\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35171:    {\r\n  descriptions.json:35172:      \"guideKey\": \"SurvivalManual\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35173:      \"displayName\": \"Stationeers Survival Manual\",   \r\n  descriptions.json:35174:      \"pageDescription\": \"&lt;b&gt;A comprehensive guide    \r\nfor new Stationeers&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\nThis survival manual covers everything you need to   \r\nknow to survive your first days on an alien world. From basic controls to       \r\nbuilding your first shelter, from managing your suit to setting up power        \r\nsystems and atmosphere - all the knowledge you need to thrive.\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35175:      \"generateToc\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35176:      \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35177:      \"buttonColor\": \"blue\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35178:      \"sortOrder\": 0,\r\n  descriptions.json:35179:      \"operationalDetails\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35180:        {\r\n  descriptions.json:35181:          \"title\": \"Part 1: Before You Choke (First   \r\n5 Minutes)\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35182:          \"tocId\": \"part1\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35183:          \"collapsible\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35184:          \"description\": \"Your journey as a\r\nStationeer begins. You're standing on an alien world with a lander nearby full  \r\nof supplies. You're already equipped with a spacesuit and tools. Don't panic -  \r\nyou have time.\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35185:          \"children\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35186:            {\r\n  descriptions.json:35187:              \"title\": \"Congratulations On Being A    \r\nStationeer!\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35188:              \"tocId\": \"congratulations\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35189:              \"collapsible\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35190:              \"description\": \"You've accepted the     \r\nchallenge. Earth needs passionate survivors who will venture out into the dark  \r\nabyss to create civilization from moon dust. You have self-selected as an       \r\nadventurer willing to take on unbelievable risks.\\n\\nYou're standing on an      \r\nalien world. There's a lander nearby with crates attached. You're already       \r\nwearing a spacesuit and holding duct tape.\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Don't panic. Take a breath     \r\n(in real life - your character is breathing just fine from their suit - for     \r\nnow).&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\nLook around with your mouse. You'll see:\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35191:              \"items\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35192:                \"&lt;b&gt;Your body&lt;/b&gt; - You're in first   \r\nperson by default. Your suit is keeping you alive. If you don't work fast now   \r\neventually it'll suffocate you when you run out of air.\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35193:                \"&lt;b&gt;The lander&lt;/b&gt; - A structure      \r\nwith several crates bolted to it. Everything you need is here.\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35194:                \"&lt;b&gt;The terrain&lt;/b&gt; - Rock, dust,     \r\nmaybe some ice/ore deposits. This is home now.\"\r\n  descriptions.json:35195:              ]\r\n  descriptions.json:35196:            },\r\n  descriptions.json:35197:            {\r\n  descriptions.json:35198:              \"title\": \"The Clock is Ticking - But    \r\nYou Have Time\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35199:              \"tocId\": \"clock-ticking\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35200:              \"collapsible\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35201:              \"description\": \"Your suit has limited   \r\noxygen, limited battery power, and limited filter life. But here's the good     \r\nnews: &lt;b&gt;you have time&lt;/b&gt;. Don't panic and rush. Mistakes from rushing kill    \r\nmore beginners than running out of air.\\n\\nYou have roughly 60 minutes of suit  \r\nsupplies if you do nothing. That's plenty of time to get oriented if you stay   \r\ncalm and move efficiently.\"\r\n  descriptions.json:35202:            },\r\n  descriptions.json:35203:            {\r\n  descriptions.json:35204:              \"title\": \"What You're Already Wearing\", \r\n  descriptions.json:35205:              \"tocId\": \"wearing\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35206:              \"collapsible\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35207:              \"description\": \"Unlike many survival    \r\ngames, you don't spawn naked. You're already fully equipped:\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35208:              \"children\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35209:                {\r\n  descriptions.json:35210:                  \"title\": \"On Your Body\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35211:                  \"tocId\": \"on-body\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35212:                  \"collapsible\": true,\r\n  descriptions.json:35213:                  \"items\": [\r\n  descriptions.json:35214:                    \"&lt;b&gt;Helmet (Press 1)&lt;/b&gt; - On     \r\nyour head, keeping good air in and bad air out. It is locked to begin with      \r\n(red button)\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35215:                    \"&lt;b&gt;EVA Suit (Press 3)&lt;/b&gt; -      \r\nContains: Air canister (5700 kPa oxygen), Waste canister, Large battery cell,   \r\n3 CO2 filters\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35216:                    \"&lt;b&gt;Jetpack (Press 4)&lt;/b&gt; - On    \r\nyour back: Propellant canister, Medical pill, Handheld tablet, Mining belt      \r\n(drill, pickaxe, 20 flares, portable solar panel)\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35217:                    \"&lt;b&gt;Orange Uniform (Press 5)&lt;/b&gt;  \r\n- Contains: Cereal Bar, Water bottle, Credit card\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35218:                    \"&lt;b&gt;Toolbelt (Press 6)&lt;/b&gt; -      \r\nContains: Wrench, Crowbar, Hand drill, Wire cutters, Welder, Screwdriver,       \r\nAngle grinder, 50 cable coils\",\r\n  descriptions.json:35219:                    \"&lt;b&gt;In Your Hand&lt;/b&gt; - Duct tape  \r\nfor repairing suit damage\"\r\n  descriptions.json:35220:                  ]\r\n\r\n\u001b[?1004h",
    "lineCount": 91
  },
  "terminalTheme": {
    "background": "#001223",
    "foreground": "#e0e0e0"
  }
```

The user is saying that the description prepend/append text from the test guide example is appearing on every Stationpedia page, not just that specific guide. This sounds like a bug in how the guide data is being applied.

Let me investigate where this could be happening. The issue is likely in how the descriptions are being rendered - perhaps there's some shared state or the guide descriptions are being applied globally instead of just to the specific guide page.

Let me search for where the prepend/append is being used.Let me investigate where the description prepend/append logic is being applied: Let me check who calls `HandlePageDescriptionModifications` and where guides are being loaded: I see the issue. The guides are being processed differently - `RenderJsonGuidePage` is called for guides, and it returns early. But the prepend/append handling happens in `RenderGuideSections` which is inside the guide rendering path.

Let me check if there's some place where guide data might be leaking into the device database: I see - guides are loaded separately via `Data.JsonGuideLoader.LoadGuides(data)`. Let me check the JsonGuideLoader to see if it's possibly putting guides into the DeviceDatabase: I see that guides are separate. Let me check if maybe the problem is in how `RegisterGuidePages()` is registering the pages - it's setting `page.Text = guide.pageDescription ?? ""` which might be affecting other pages. Let me look at the Stationpedia.Register method to understand how this works:

Actually, wait - let me look at the descriptions.json file to see if maybe the test guide is accidentally being set as a device rather than a guide: I found the problem. The test data is set as a `device` with `deviceKey: "ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator"` which is an actual device key in the game. This means when you look at ANY Solid Fuel Generator page, it's applying the test modifications.

The prepend text `"GUIDE FORMAT ADDITIONS TEST PAGE"` is being applied via `HandlePageDescriptionModifications` to the actual Solid Fuel Generator device page.

But you said it's appearing on "every" page, which is strange. Let me check if there's something else going on - perhaps the description is being cached or applied more broadly: Let me check line 37050 context: I see! There's a guide with `"deviceKey": "NewGuide"` in the `guides` array at around line 37049. Let me get a cleaner view: Let me look at around line 37049 for the "NewGuide": I see the issue! There's a guide with `"deviceKey": "NewGuide"` in the guides array, but it ALSO has the `pageDescriptionPrepend` and `pageDescriptionAppend` fields that are getting incorrectly applied somewhere.

Wait - this is weird. Guides use `guideKey`, not `deviceKey`. Let me see more context: Now I see the FULL picture. The `NewGuide` entry at line 37028 CORRECTLY has `guideKey`, but then at line 37049 it ALSO has a `deviceKey` field set to "NewGuide". This is a duplicate/malformed entry in the same object!

The problem is that `deviceKey` is being read by the device handling code (not the guide code), and it's probably trying to find a device called "NewGuide" and applying prepend/append to... something weird.

But let me also check the first device entry - the one at line 6: I found the root cause! There's a device entry `deviceKey: "ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator"` with test data including `pageDescriptionPrepend`. This is a REAL device in the game, so the prepend text is being applied to the actual Solid Fuel Generator page!

Let me check how HandlePageDescriptionModifications works: The code looks right - it's supposed to only apply to the SPECIFIC device. The issue is simply that the test data in `descriptions.json` uses a REAL device key `ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator`.

Let me check where the descriptions are loaded and matched: So the issue is clear: **The user's test data in `descriptions.json` is using a REAL device key**, so the prepend text is being applied to the actual Solid Fuel Generator page in the game.

This isn't a bug in the code - it's the test data that needs to be cleaned up. The user should:
1. Remove the test data from `ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator` or change it to a non-existent key like `TestDevice`
2. Fix the `NewGuide` entry that has both `guideKey` AND `deviceKey` fields

Let me explain this to the user clearly:

---

I found the issue! It's not a code bug - **your test data in descriptions.json is using a real device key**.

## The Problem

In your `undefined`, the first device entry is:
```json
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator",
  "displayName": "GUIDE FORMAT TEST - Solid Fuel Generator",
  "pageDescriptionPrepend": "<color=#00FF00><b>GUIDE FORMAT ADDITIONS TEST PAGE</b></color>..."
}
```

`ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator` is a **real device** in the game! So when you view the actual Solid Fuel Generator in Stationpedia, the prepend text gets applied to it.

## Additionally

Your `NewGuide` guide entry at `undefined` has some issues:
- It has BOTH `guideKey: "NewGuide"` (correct) AND `deviceKey: "NewGuide"` (incorrect duplicate field at line 37049)
- It has `flatStructure: true` which is an obsolete field

## Fix Options

**Option A (Recommended):** Clean up the test data - change `ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator` to something like `TestDevice_DoNotUse` that doesn't exist in the game.

**Option B:** Remove the test data entirely if you no longer need it for testing.

Would you like me to:
1. Clean up the test data by renaming the device key to a non-existent key?
2. Remove the test data entirely?
3. Just fix the `NewGuide` entry's duplicate `deviceKey` field?

Ah, I understand now! The issue is that when the user views a guide page, the prepend/append text is being applied, and then when they navigate to OTHER pages (not just the guide), that prepend/append text persists on those other pages too.

This sounds like a state persistence issue - the prepend/append text is being added to the page description but not being reset when navigating to a different page.

Let me look at the code flow to understand how this happens:

1. User opens a guide page
2. RenderGuideSections adds prepend/append text to the page description
3. User navigates to another page
4. The page description still has the old prepend/append text

The issue is likely that the page description text isn't being reset when navigating between pages, OR the prepend/append is being applied in a way that accumulates.

Let me look at the RenderGuideSections function to see how it handles prepend/append for guides:Ah, now I understand! The prepend/append text is persisting across page navigation. This is a state issue - when you apply prepend/append to a guide page, it's modifying the shared page description, and that modification persists when you navigate to other pages.

Let me look at how RenderGuideSections handles prepend/append: I see! The guide rendering uses `CreateTextElement` to render the description with prepend/append - that's correct and doesn't modify the original page description.

But let me check how device pages handle prepend/append - the issue might be there. Let me look at HandlePageDescriptionModifications again and where it's called: Now I see! `HandlePageDescriptionModifications` is modifying `page.PageDescription.text` directly! When you navigate to another page, the PageDescription component might be reused with the modified text.

Let me check how the vanilla code works - the page description might need to be reset: Now let me check how guides call HandlePageDescriptionModifications or if they do something different. Let me search for where guides apply prepend/append: The guide code at line 558 correctly uses a local variable `pageDescText` and creates a new text element. That's fine.

But wait - for custom guides, we're rendering custom content... but what about when you LEAVE a guide and go to another page? Let me check if guides are hiding the original page description: Now let me check how the `page.PageDescription` text is being handled. I wonder if the guide is modifying it somewhere. Let me look at the UniversalPage_Open_Patch to see the full flow: I see the flow now. For JSON guides, it calls `RenderJsonGuidePage` and returns. The issue might be that guides ARE modifying the page description somewhere.

Wait, let me look at what happens in RenderGuideSections with page descriptions more carefully - specifically if there's any code that modifies `page.PageDescription.text` directly for guides: These are only in `HandlePageDescriptionModifications` for DEVICE pages, not guides.

Let me check if `RenderJsonGuidePage` or `RenderGuideSections` might be hiding the original page description in a way that doesn't restore properly: Hmm, let me look at the `CreateTextElement` function to understand how it adds text to guides: Wait, I see it now! The `CreateTextElement` uses `sourceText` which is `page.PageDescription` - this is used as a TEMPLATE to copy font/style properties, but doesn't modify it. That's fine.

Let me think about this differently. The user says the prepend/append text persists "across any other pages". This means after viewing a guide with prepend/append, when they navigate to another page (even a vanilla page), the prepend/append text is still visible.

Wait - I need to understand WHERE the prepend/append gets rendered for guides. Let me look at line 568:

```csharp
CreateTextElement(containerRT, sourceText, pageDescText.Trim());
```

This creates a NEW text element in `containerRT` (the "GuideSectionsContent" container). But that container is destroyed when navigating away (line 525-528 in RenderGuideSections).

OH WAIT! I think I understand. The issue might be that `page.PageDescription` itself isn't being touched - but when you open a VANILLA guide page that doesn't have custom data, the vanilla page description might be getting the prepend/append from somewhere...

Let me check if guides in the JSON are also registered in the DeviceDatabase: Now let me check the JsonGuideLoader to see how it handles prepend/append and if there's any interaction with DeviceDatabase: Now I see the flow! When a guide page is registered via `RegisterGuidePages()`, it sets `page.Text = guide.pageDescription`. 

But wait, that's the `StationpediaPage.Text` which is just the data for the page. The actual page rendering happens through `ChangeDisplay_Postfix`.

Let me check where vanilla guides (like Airlock Guide) get their page description text. When you click on a vanilla guide, does it also go through our `ChangeDisplay_Postfix`? Let me look at the vanilla guide page keys to understand the flow. Let me also search for how the page description gets populated: Now I understand! Look at the guide rendering path:

1. User opens a JSON guide (like "NewGuide")
2. `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` is called with the guide's page
3. `HasGuide(pageKey)` returns true, so it calls `RenderJsonGuidePage`
4. `RenderJsonGuidePage` creates guide content including the prepend/append text as NEW elements (via `CreateTextElement`)

But... the problem is on line 68 of JsonGuideLoader.cs:
```csharp
page.Text = guide.pageDescription ?? "";
```

This sets the TEXT of the StationpediaPage to the guide's pageDescription. But that's separate from prepend/append.

Wait - let me re-read the issue. The user says when clicking a guide like "Airlock Guide", the prepend and append text persists. Is "Airlock Guide" a vanilla guide or a custom JSON guide?

Let me check if there's any interaction with vanilla guides. Let me see how vanilla guides are rendered - do they use our patches?

Let me look at what happens when a vanilla guide that doesn't have custom data goes through the patch: Hmm, so for vanilla guides (not in `JsonGuideLoader` and not SurvivalManual or DaylightSensorGuide):
1. They still go through `ChangeDisplay_Postfix`
2. They check if there's data in `DeviceDatabase` 
3. If not, it just returns

But wait... I need to check if any guide keys might accidentally match something in the DeviceDatabase!

Let me check the structure of `descriptions.json` again: I see! The test device data has `pageDescriptionPrepend` on `ThingStructureSolidFuelGenerator`. This device data ALSO goes into the DeviceDatabase. 

Wait, but the user says it persists across "any other pages" - so viewing the Solid Fuel Generator page would apply the prepend text to that page, but then viewing another page (like Airlock) shouldn't keep it because that's a completely different page...

Unless... there's a state issue with how UniversalPage works. Let me think about this:

1. User opens a page with prepend/append (e.g., Solid Fuel Generator with test data)
2. `HandlePageDescriptionModifications` modifies `page.PageDescription.text` directly
3. User navigates to another page (e.g., Airlock Guide)
4. The same UniversalPage instance might be reused, but the vanilla code SHOULD reset `PageDescription.text`

OR... the issue could be that the text we added via prepend/append is still in the text because the vanilla code doesn't fully reset it.

Let me check if there's some issue with how the vanilla text gets reset. The vanilla game should set `PageDescription.text` to the new page's text, but maybe it's not?

Actually, I think I understand now! The problem might be:

1. Custom guide "NewGuide" has prepend/append
2. The guide's `pageDescription` is rendered via `CreateTextElement` which ADDS elements to the container
3. But wait - guides also hide/show elements...

Let me look more carefully at what RenderGuideSections does with the original page description: I see! The guide rendering creates elements in a container called "GuideSectionsContent". This container is destroyed and recreated each time a guide is rendered (lines 525-528):

```csharp
var existingContent = contentTransform.Find("GuideSectionsContent");
if (existingContent != null)
{
    UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(existingContent.gameObject);
}
```

So the custom content shouldn't persist. Unless...

Wait, let me check if the VANILLA page description element is being modified. When the guide renders, does it ALSO modify `page.PageDescription.text`?

Looking at the code:
- `sourceText = page.PageDescription` - just gets a reference to use as a style template
- `CreateTextElement(containerRT, sourceText, pageDescText.Trim())` - creates a NEW element with the combined text

So the vanilla `page.PageDescription` element should NOT be modified by guide rendering...

BUT WAIT! Look at this in JsonGuideLoader line 68:
```csharp
page.Text = guide.pageDescription ?? "";
```

This is setting the `StationpediaPage.Text` property when REGISTERING the guide page. But that's the data object, not the UI element...

Hmm, let me think about this from a different angle. What if the problem is that when you navigate AWAY from a custom guide and go to a vanilla page, the vanilla code copies the StationpediaPage.Text to page.PageDescription.text... but our mod has already registered guides with prepend/append?

Actually no, the prepend/append isn't stored in `page.Text`.

Let me add some debugging. Actually, let me just look at what the vanilla ChangeDisplay does and how our patch interacts.

Wait - I have another theory! Look at the flow again:

1. User opens custom guide "NewGuide" which has prepend/append
2. `RenderJsonGuidePage` is called, which calls `RenderGuideSections`
3. `RenderGuideSections` creates "GuideSectionsContent" container with the prepend/append text
4. User navigates to "Airlock Guide" (vanilla guide)
5. ChangeDisplay is called for "Airlock Guide"
6. Our `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` runs
7. `HasGuide("AirlockGuide")` returns FALSE (it's not a custom JSON guide)
8. `DeviceDatabase.TryGetValue("AirlockGuide", ...)` - does this return something?

Let me check if the "NewGuide" entry in descriptions.json has BOTH `guideKey` AND `deviceKey`! I saw this earlier:

```json
Line 37029: "guideKey": "NewGuide",
Line 37049: "deviceKey": "NewGuide",
```

So "NewGuide" has BOTH a `guideKey` (for guides array) AND a `deviceKey` field that's incorrectly inside the same object! This means "NewGuide" might ALSO be registered in the DeviceDatabase!

Let me check how the devices are loaded: OK so `DeviceDatabase` is populated from `data.devices` array. The guides are loaded separately via `Data.JsonGuideLoader.LoadGuides(data)`.

But the malformed `NewGuide` entry has a `deviceKey` field inside a guide object. Let me check how guides are parsed: OK so `GuideDescription` has a `guideKey` field, and `DeviceDescriptions` has a `deviceKey` field. They are separate types.

But looking at the JSON again, the `NewGuide` entry in the `guides` array has BOTH:
- Line 37029: `"guideKey": "NewGuide"`
- Line 37049: `"deviceKey": "NewGuide"`

When JSON.NET deserializes this into a `GuideDescription`, the `deviceKey` field would just be ignored (unless there's a property for it, which there isn't).

So the actual issue must be different. Let me think again...

Actually, let me re-read the user's message: "when I click a guide like airlock guide or any guide the prepend and apend text persists across any other pages as well"

So the user is saying:
1. Click a guide (like Airlock Guide - this could be vanilla or custom)
2. The prepend/append text appears (from some guide that HAS prepend/append)
3. Navigate to another page (any page)
4. The prepend/append text is STILL visible

This means the text is persisting across navigation. This sounds like the text content isn't being cleaned up properly.

Let me think about this from a different angle. When a custom JSON guide is rendered, we create "GuideSectionsContent" and add the prepend/append text there. When the user navigates away, does this container get cleaned up?

Let me check if there's something that cleans up "GuideSectionsContent" when navigating to a non-guide page:

The issue might be:
1. Open custom guide -> GuideSectionsContent is created with prepend/append text
2. Navigate to vanilla guide (like Airlock) -> Our patch runs but Airlock isn't in JsonGuideLoader
3. The old GuideSectionsContent might still exist!

Let me verify - when you navigate to a vanilla page, what cleans up the "GuideSectionsContent"?

Looking at the code:
- `RenderGuideSections` at line 525-528 destroys "GuideSectionsContent" BEFORE creating a new one
- But `RenderGuideSections` is only called for custom JSON guides!
- For vanilla guides that don't have custom data, the `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` just returns without cleaning up!

**THAT'S THE BUG!**

When you navigate from a custom JSON guide to a vanilla page:
1. `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` runs
2. It doesn't match SurvivalManual, JsonGuideLoader, DaylightSensorGuide
3. It doesn't find anything in DeviceDatabase
4. It returns WITHOUT cleaning up "GuideSectionsContent" from the previous custom guide!

The fix is to ALWAYS clean up "GuideSectionsContent" at the start of `ChangeDisplay_Postfix`, not just inside `RenderGuideSections`.

Let me fix this: Now I understand the structure. I need to add cleanup of "GuideSectionsContent" right after getting the content transform, BEFORE any of the special page handling. This way, navigating to ANY page will clean up the leftover guide content. Now let me also clean up any leftover "SurvivalManualContent" container for the same reason: Let me also add cleanup for "SurvivalManualContent" and "OperationalDetailsCategory" in the same place: Now let me build and verify: Build succeeded. 

**Summary of the fix:**

The bug was that when navigating from a custom JSON guide (which creates "GuideSectionsContent" container with prepend/append text) to a vanilla page or different guide, the old container was NOT being cleaned up. This caused the prepend/append text to persist visually.

**The fix:** Added cleanup of leftover content containers (`GuideSectionsContent`, `SurvivalManualContent`, `OperationalDetailsCategory`) at the START of `ChangeDisplay_Postfix`, BEFORE any page-specific rendering. This ensures that navigating to ANY page will clean up leftover content from the previous page, preventing text from persisting across navigation.

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nGuard against null operationalDetails\r\n                if (guideData.operationalDetails == null || guideData.operationalDetails.Count == 0)\r\n                {\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Create outer container that will hold the TOC box\r\n                var outerGO = new GameObject(\"GuideTOCOuter\");\r\n                var outerRT = outerGO.AddComponent&lt;RectTransform&gt;();\r\n                outerGO.transform.SetParent(parent, false);\r\n                \r\n                outerRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);\r\n                outerRT.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);\r\n                outerRT.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);\r\n                \r\n                // Auto-size height for outer\r\n                var outerFitter = outerGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter&gt;();\r\n                outerFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;\r\n                outerFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                \r\n                // Create TOC container with background\r\n                var tocGO = new GameObject(\"GuideTOC\");\r\n                tocGO.transform.SetParent(outerGO.transform, false);\r\n                \r\n                // Add background panel - matches device TOC style\r\n                var bgImage = tocGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.Image&gt;();\r\n                if (VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)\r\n                {\r\n                    if (_nativePanelSprite != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        bgImage.sprite = _nativePanelSprite;\r\n                        bgImage.type = _nativePanelType;\r\n                        bgImage.material = _nativePanelMaterial;\r\n                    }\r\n                    bgImage.color = new Color(0.15f, 0.15f, 0.15f, 0.95f);\r\n                }\r\n                else\r\n                {\r\n                    var windowSprite = StationpediaAscendedMod.GetWindowBackgroundSprite();\r\n                    if (windowSprite != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        bgImage.sprite = windowSprite;\r\n                        bgImage.type = UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Sliced;\r\n                    }\r\n                    bgImage.color = new Color(0.05f, 0.1f, 0.2f, 0.95f);\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Add padding layout for TOC content - constrain max width to stay within bounds\r\n                var tocLayout = tocGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup&gt;();\r\n                tocLayout.padding = new RectOffset(16, 16, 12, 12);\r\n                tocLayout.spacing = 6;\r\n                tocLayout.childForceExpandWidth = false;\r\n                tocLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;\r\n                tocLayout.childControlWidth = true;\r\n                tocLayout.childControlHeight = true;\r\n                \r\n                // Add LayoutElement to constrain max width\r\n                var tocLayoutElement = tocGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement&gt;();\r\n                tocLayoutElement.flexibleWidth = 0f;\r\n                tocLayoutElement.minWidth = 150f;\r\n                \r\n                // Auto-size for TOC - fit content but respect max width\r\n                var tocFitter = tocGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter&gt;();\r\n                tocFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                tocFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                \r\n                // Create title\r\n                var titleGO = new GameObject(\"TOCTitle\");\r\n                titleGO.transform.SetParent(tocGO.transform, false);\r\n                var titleText = titleGO.AddComponent&lt;TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI&gt;();\r\n                titleText.font = sourceText.font;\r\n                titleText.fontSharedMaterial = sourceText.fontSharedMaterial;\r\n                titleText.fontSize = sourceText.fontSize * 0.9f;\r\n                titleText.color = VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode ? Color.white : new Color(1f, 0.6f, 0.2f, 1f);\r\n                titleText.text = string.IsNullOrEmpty(guideData.tocTitle) ? \"&lt;b&gt;Contents&lt;/b&gt;\" : $\"&lt;b&gt;{guideData.tocTitle}&lt;/b&gt;\";\r\n                titleText.enableWordWrapping = false;\r\n                \r\n                var titleFitter = titleGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter&gt;();\r\n                titleFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                \r\n                // Collect all TOC entries - use tocFlat option to control nesting\r\n                var tocEntries = new List&lt;(string tocId, string title, int depth)&gt;();\r\n                foreach (var detail in guideData.operationalDetails)\r\n                {\r\n                    CollectTocEntriesForGuide(tocEntries, detail, 0, guideData.tocFlat);\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Calculate columns - same logic as device TOC\r\n                const int MAX_ROWS = 8;\r\n                int totalEntries = tocEntries.Count;\r\n                int numColumns = (totalEntries + MAX_ROWS - 1) / MAX_ROWS;\r\n                numColumns = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(3, numColumns)); // 1-3 columns max\r\n                \r\n                // Create horizontal container for columns\r\n                var columnsGO = new GameObject(\"TOCColumns\");\r\n                columnsGO.transform.SetParent(tocGO.transform, false);\r\n                \r\n                var columnsLayout = columnsGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.HorizontalLayoutGroup&gt;();\r\n                columnsLayout.spacing = 20;\r\n                columnsLayout.childForceExpandWidth = false;\r\n                columnsLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;\r\n                columnsLayout.childControlWidth = true;\r\n                columnsLayout.childControlHeight = true;\r\n                columnsLayout.childAlignment = UnityEngine.TextAnchor.UpperLeft;\r\n                \r\n                var columnsFitter = columnsGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter&gt;();\r\n                columnsFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                columnsFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                \r\n                // Create each column\r\n                int entryIndex = 0;\r\n                for (int col = 0; col &lt; numColumns &amp;&amp; entryIndex &lt; totalEntries; col++)\r\n                {\r\n                    var columnGO = new GameObject($\"TOCColumn{col}\");\r\n                    columnGO.transform.SetParent(columnsGO.transform, false);\r\n                    \r\n                    var linksText = columnGO.AddComponent&lt;TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI&gt;();\r\n                    linksText.font = sourceText.font;\r\n                    linksText.fontSharedMaterial = sourceText.fontSharedMaterial;\r\n                    linksText.fontSize = sourceText.fontSize * 0.85f;\r\n                    linksText.color = sourceText.color;\r\n                    linksText.enableWordWrapping = false;\r\n                    linksText.richText = true;\r\n                    linksText.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Build this column's entries\r\n                    var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();\r\n                    int rowsInColumn = 0;\r\n                    while (entryIndex &lt; totalEntries &amp;&amp; rowsInColumn &lt; MAX_ROWS)\r\n                    {\r\n                        var entry = tocEntries[entryIndex];\r\n                        string indent = entry.depth &gt; 0 ? new string(' ', entry.depth * 2) : \"\";\r\n                        string bullet = entry.depth &gt; 0 ? \"- \" : \"\";\r\n                        string bulletColor = VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode ? \"#888888\" : \"#CC6600\";\r\n                        string textColor = \"#FFFFFF\";\r\n                        \r\n                        if (entry.depth &gt; 0)\r\n                        {\r\n                            sb.AppendLine($\"{indent}&lt;color={bulletColor}&gt;{bullet}&lt;/color&gt;&lt;link=toc_{entry.tocId}&gt;&lt;color={textColor}&gt;{entry.title}&lt;/color&gt;&lt;/link&gt;\");\r\n                        }\r\n                        else\r\n                        {\r\n                            sb.AppendLine($\"&lt;link=toc_{entry.tocId}&gt;&lt;color={textColor}&gt;{entry.title}&lt;/color&gt;&lt;/link&gt;\");\r\n                        }\r\n                        \r\n                        entryIndex++;\r\n                        rowsInColumn++;\r\n                    }\r\n                    linksText.text = sb.ToString().TrimEnd();\r\n                    \r\n                    // Add LayoutElement to limit column width\r\n                    var colLayoutElement = columnGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement&gt;();\r\n                    colLayoutElement.preferredWidth = 180f; // Limit column width\r\n                    \r\n                    var linksFitter = columnGO.AddComponent&lt;UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter&gt;();\r\n                    linksFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                    linksFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Add click handler for TOC links\r\n                    var linkHandler = columnGO.AddComponent&lt;TocLinkHandler&gt;();\r\n                    linkHandler.TextComponent = linksText;\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error creating guide TOC: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Collect TOC entries for guides - supports flat mode (no nesting)\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void CollectTocEntriesForGuide(List&lt;(string tocId, string title, int depth)&gt; entries, OperationalDetail detail, int depth, bool flatMode)\r\n        {\r\n            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.tocId) &amp;&amp; !string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.title))\r\n            {\r\n                // In flat mode, all entries are at depth 0\r\n                int effectiveDepth = flatMode ? 0 : depth;\r\n                entries.Add((detail.tocId, detail.title, effectiveDepth));\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            if (detail.children != null)\r\n            {\r\n                foreach (var child in detail.children)\r\n                {\r\n                    CollectTocEntriesForGuide(entries, child, depth + 1, flatMode);\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Configure layout for TOC category (legacy - kept for compatibility)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
```

---

The user wants:
1. Guide TOCs to have the same 3-column format as device TOCs (currently guides have a single line TOC)
2. TOCs to stay within their dark background (not stretch beyond edges)
3. Option for non-nested TOC in guides

Let me first look at how device TOCs are created vs guide TOCs to understand the difference.Let me look at how device TOCs are created compared to guide TOCs: Now let me look at how device TOCs are created - specifically the 3-column grid format: Now I understand the difference:
1. **Device TOC** (`CreateTableOfContents`): Uses multi-column layout with MAX_ROWS=8, collects entries, arranges in columns
2. **Guide TOC** (`CreateGuideTableOfContents`): Uses `BuildTocContent` which just adds entries one by one vertically

Now let me check the `Models.cs` to add a `tocFlat` option: Now I need to:
1. Add `tocFlat` property to both `GuideDescription` and `DeviceDescriptions`
2. Update `JsonGuideLoader.ToDeviceDescriptions` to copy this property
3. Rewrite `CreateGuideTableOfContents` to use the same column-based layout as `CreateTableOfContents`
4. Add width constraints to prevent TOC from stretching beyond edges Now update JsonGuideLoader to copy the tocFlat property: Now I need to rewrite `CreateGuideTableOfContents` to use the same multi-column layout as device TOCs. Let me also look at how guides call the TOC creation to pass the flat parameter: Now let me completely rewrite the `CreateGuideTableOfContents` function to use the same column-based layout as device TOCs: Now let me see the rest of `BuildTocContent` and `ConfigureTocCategoryLayout`: Now I'll rewrite `CreateGuideTableOfContents` to match the device TOC layout. I'll replace the entire function with a new version that uses the column-based layout: Now I need to also add max-width constraints to the device TOC to prevent it from stretching beyond the stationpedia edges. Let me look at the device TOC code again: I see the device TOC doesn't limit column width. Let me add a max-width to each column there too, and also add a max width constraint on the outer container. Let me find where the columns are created: Now I need to add a LayoutElement to device TOC columns to limit their width, and also add a max width to the overall TOC container. Let me update the device TOC: Now let me also add a max width to the TOC container itself to prevent overflow. Let me find and update that section: Now let me also update the device TOC to support the `tocFlat` option. Let me find where CollectTocEntries is called: Now let me update the device TOC to use the same flat mode support. I'll update `CollectTocEntries` to accept a flat parameter and update the call site: Now let me update the `CollectTocEntries` function to support flat mode, and update the device TOC call site: Now update the device TOC call site to pass the tocFlat parameter: Now I can remove the duplicate `CollectTocEntriesForGuide` function since `CollectTocEntries` now handles flat mode. Let me find and remove it: Let me update the guide TOC to use the shared `CollectTocEntries` function instead: The `BuildTocContent` function is no longer used by the guide TOC. Let me remove it: Build succeeded. Let me also check if ConfigureTocCategoryLayout is still used anywhere: It's now unused. Let me remove it too: Build succeeded. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Guide TOC now uses same multi-column format as Device TOC
- Rewrote `CreateGuideTableOfContents` to use the same column-based layout system as device TOCs
- TOC entries are now arranged in up to 3 columns with 8 rows max per column
- Uses the same color scheme (white text, orange bullets in Ascended mode, grey bullets in vanilla)
- Has proper dark background matching the device TOC style

### 2. TOC entries now stay within their background bounds
- Added `preferredWidth` constraint (180px) to each column to prevent overflow
- Added `preferredWidth` constraint (620px) to the overall TOC container for devices
- Guide TOC uses the same constraints

### 3. Added `tocFlat` option for non-nested TOC
- Added `tocFlat` property to both `GuideDescription` and `DeviceDescriptions` models
- When `tocFlat: true`, all TOC entries are rendered at the same level (no indentation for children)
- This applies to both guides and devices

### Usage in JSON:
```json
{
  "guideKey": "MyGuide",
  "displayName": "My Guide",
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "Jump To",
  "tocFlat": true,  // NEW - makes all entries flat (no nesting)
  "operationalDetails": [...]
}
```

---

Looking at the screenshot, I can see the TOC is overlapping the content below it. The TOC entries are rendering but the container isn't pushing the content below it down properly. This is likely a layout issue where the TOC container isn't being properly included in the vertical layout flow.

Let me look at the guide TOC code to understand the issue. The problem seems to be that the outer container isn't properly sized or positioned.

Also, the user wants a button in the editor to toggle nested TOC. Let me look at the editor code for guides.I see the issue - the TOC is overlapping the content below it. The container isn't properly pushing down the content. Let me fix the layout and also add the nested TOC toggle to the editor. I see the issue. The outer container needs a proper layout element to participate in the parent's vertical layout. Let me fix this and also look at the editor to add the nested toggle: Now let me find the editor file to add the TOC nested toggle: Let me see more around the TOC section where I need to add the nested checkbox: Now I'll fix both issues - the TOC layout and add the nested toggle. First, let me update the C# code to fix the layout overlap: Now let me add the "Nested TOC" checkbox to the editor. I need to add it to both sections - for guides: Now let me also add it to the device TOC settings section (line 1401 area): Now I need to add `tocFlat` to the TypeScript types. Let me find the types file: Now let me also update the `stationpedia.ts` types: Now let me also update the simulator types file: Now let me also look at the ResolvedDevicePage interface to add tocFlat there: Now update the `stationpediaService.ts` to include tocFlat: Now let me build the C# mod: Build succeeded. Let me verify the editor type changes don't have errors: No errors. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary

### 1. Fixed TOC Layout Overlap
- Added `LayoutElement` and `HorizontalLayoutGroup` to the outer TOC container so it properly participates in the parent's vertical layout
- Added bottom padding (10px) to create separation between TOC and content below
- The TOC now properly pushes down subsequent content instead of overlapping

### 2. Added "Nested" Toggle in Editor
- Added a "Nested" checkbox next to "Generate Table of Contents" in both:
  - Guide editor section
  - Device Operational Details section
- When checked (default), TOC entries show nested indentation for child sections
- When unchecked (`tocFlat: true`), all TOC entries appear at the same level

### 3. Updated TypeScript Types
Added `tocFlat?: boolean` to:
- `DeviceDocument` interface
- `GuideDocument` interface  
- `DeviceDescription` interface
- `DeviceOverride` interface
- `ResolvedDevicePage` interface
- `stationpediaService` resolver

The checkbox label says "Nested" and when checked it sets `tocFlat: false` (nested mode), when unchecked it sets `tocFlat: true` (flat mode).

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="copilot_replaceString">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_replaceString**</summary>

Additional data:

```json
{
  "vscodeToolId": "copilot_replaceString",
  "isHidden": true
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\ns for PNG files (Texture2D and thumbnails)\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:scanImageFolder', async (_, baseFolder?: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Base path to AssetRipperFiles\r\n    const basePaths = [\r\n      baseFolder ? path.join(baseFolder, '..', 'AssetRipperFiles', 'ExportedProject', 'Assets') : null,\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\AssetRipperFiles\\\\ExportedProject\\\\Assets',\r\n    ].filter(Boolean) as string[];\r\n\r\n    let assetsPath: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const p of basePaths) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(p)) {\r\n        assetsPath = p;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!assetsPath) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find Assets folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Folders to scan for PNGs\r\n    const foldersToScan = [\r\n      { path: path.join(assetsPath, 'Resources', 'ui', 'thumbnails'), category: 'Thumbnails' },\r\n      { path: path.join(assetsPath, 'Texture2D'), category: 'UI Textures' },\r\n    ];\r\n\r\n    const images: { filename: string; path: string; category: string }[] = [];\r\n\r\n    for (const folder of foldersToScan) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(folder.path)) {\r\n        const entries = fs.readdirSync(folder.path, { withFileTypes: true });\r\n        for (const entry of entries) {\r\n          if (entry.isFile() &amp;&amp; entry.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.png')) {\r\n            images.push({\r\n              filename: entry.name,\r\n              path: path.join(folder.path, entry.name),\r\n              category: folder.category,\r\n            });\r\n          }\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Sort alphabetically by filename\r\n    images.sort((a, b) =&gt; a.filename.localeCompare(b.filename));\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, images, folder: assets\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "s for PNG files (Texture2D and thumbnails)\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:scanImageFolder', async (_, baseFolder?: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Base path to AssetRipperFiles\r\n    const basePaths = [\r\n      baseFolder ? path.join(baseFolder, '..', 'AssetRipperFiles', 'ExportedProject', 'Assets') : null,\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\AssetRipperFiles\\\\ExportedProject\\\\Assets',\r\n    ].filter(Boolean) as string[];\r\n\r\n    let assetsPath: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const p of basePaths) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(p)) {\r\n        assetsPath = p;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!assetsPath) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find Assets folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Folders to scan for PNGs\r\n    const foldersToScan = [\r\n      { path: path.join(assetsPath, 'Resources', 'ui', 'thumbnails'), category: 'Thumbnails' },\r\n      { path: path.join(assetsPath, 'Texture2D'), category: 'UI Textures' },\r\n    ];\r\n\r\n    const images: { filename: string; path: string; category: string }[] = [];\r\n\r\n    for (const folder of foldersToScan) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(folder.path)) {\r\n        const entries = fs.readdirSync(folder.path, { withFileTypes: true });\r\n        for (const entry of entries) {\r\n          if (entry.isFile() &amp;&amp; entry.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.png')) {\r\n            images.push({\r\n              filename: entry.name,\r\n              path: path.join(folder.path, entry.name),\r\n              category: folder.category,\r\n            });\r\n          }\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Sort alphabetically by filename\r\n    images.sort((a, b) =&gt; a.filename.localeCompare(b.filename));\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, images, folder: assets",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n\r\n\r\n// Copy image to mod images folder\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:copyImageToMod', async (_, sourcePath: string, filename: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Default mod images folder\r\n    const modImagesFolders = [\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\StationpediaAscended\\\\mod\\\\images',\r\n    ];\r\n\r\n    let targetFolder: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const folder of modImagesFolders) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(folder)) {\r\n        targetFolder = folder;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!targetFolder) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find mod images folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const targetPath = path.join(targetFolder, filename);\r\n\r\n    // Check if file already exists\r\n    if (fs.existsSync(targetPath)) {\r\n      return { success: true, alreadyExists: true, targetPath };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Copy the file\r\n    fs.copyFileSync(sourcePath, targetPath);\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, copied: true, targetPath };\r\n  } catch (error: any) {\r\n    return { success: false, error: error.message };\r\n  }\r\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "\r\n\r\n// Copy image to mod images folder\r\nipcMain.handle('fs:copyImageToMod', async (_, sourcePath: string, filename: string) =&gt; {\r\n  try {\r\n    // Default mod images folder\r\n    const modImagesFolders = [\r\n      'C:\\\\Dev\\\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\\StationpediaAscended\\\\mod\\\\images',\r\n    ];\r\n\r\n    let targetFolder: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const folder of modImagesFolders) {\r\n      if (fs.existsSync(folder)) {\r\n        targetFolder = folder;\r\n        break;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!targetFolder) {\r\n      return { success: false, error: 'Could not find mod images folder' };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const targetPath = path.join(targetFolder, filename);\r\n\r\n    // Check if file already exists\r\n    if (fs.existsSync(targetPath)) {\r\n      return { success: true, alreadyExists: true, targetPath };\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Copy the file\r\n    fs.copyFileSync(sourcePath, targetPath);\r\n\r\n    return { success: true, copied: true, targetPath };\r\n  } catch (error: any) {\r\n    return { success: false, error: error.message };\r\n  }\r\n});",
```

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\n - copy to mod folder and return filename\r\n  const handleSelect = async () =&gt; {\r\n    if (selectedImage) {\r\n      const imageInfo = images.find(i =&gt; i.filename === selectedImage);\r\n      if (imageInfo) {\r\n        setCopyStatus('Copying...');\r\n        try {\r\n          const result = await window.electronAPI?.copyImageToMod?.(imageInfo.path, imageInfo.filename);\r\n          if (result?.success) {\r\n            if (result.copied) {\r\n              setCopyStatus('✓ Copied to mod images folder');\r\n            } else if (result.alreadyExists) {\r\n              setCopyStatus('✓ Already exists in mod images folder');\r\n            }\r\n            // Brief delay to show status, then close\r\n            setTimeout(() =&gt; {\r\n              onSelectImage(selectedImage);\r\n              onClose();\r\n            }, 500);\r\n          } else {\r\n            setCopyStatus(&#96;⚠️ Copy failed: ${result?.error || 'Unknown error'}&#96;);\r\n          }\r\n        } catch (err) {\r\n          setCopyStatus(&#96;⚠️ Copy failed: ${err}&#96;);\r\n        }\r\n      } else {\r\n        onSelectImage(selectedImage);\r\n        onClose();\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  // Handle double-click to select immediately\r\n  const handleDoubleClick = async (filename: string) =&gt; {\r\n    const imageInfo = images.find(i =&gt; i.filename === filename);\r\n    if (imageInfo) {\r\n      try {\r\n        await window.electronAPI?.copyImageToMod?.(imageInfo.path, imageInfo.filename);\r\n      } catch (err) {\r\n        console.warn('Failed to copy image:', err);\r\n      }\r\n    }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": " - copy to mod folder and return filename\r\n  const handleSelect = async () =&gt; {\r\n    if (selectedImage) {\r\n      const imageInfo = images.find(i =&gt; i.filename === selectedImage);\r\n      if (imageInfo) {\r\n        setCopyStatus('Copying...');\r\n        try {\r\n          const result = await window.electronAPI?.copyImageToMod?.(imageInfo.path, imageInfo.filename);\r\n          if (result?.success) {\r\n            if (result.copied) {\r\n              setCopyStatus('✓ Copied to mod images folder');\r\n            } else if (result.alreadyExists) {\r\n              setCopyStatus('✓ Already exists in mod images folder');\r\n            }\r\n            // Brief delay to show status, then close\r\n            setTimeout(() =&gt; {\r\n              onSelectImage(selectedImage);\r\n              onClose();\r\n            }, 500);\r\n          } else {\r\n            setCopyStatus(&#96;⚠️ Copy failed: ${result?.error || 'Unknown error'}&#96;);\r\n          }\r\n        } catch (err) {\r\n          setCopyStatus(&#96;⚠️ Copy failed: ${err}&#96;);\r\n        }\r\n      } else {\r\n        onSelectImage(selectedImage);\r\n        onClose();\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  // Handle double-click to select immediately\r\n  const handleDoubleClick = async (filename: string) =&gt; {\r\n    const imageInfo = images.find(i =&gt; i.filename === filename);\r\n    if (imageInfo) {\r\n      try {\r\n        await window.electronAPI?.copyImageToMod?.(imageInfo.path, imageInfo.filename);\r\n      } catch (err) {\r\n        console.warn('Failed to copy image:', err);\r\n      }\r\n    }",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
