Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaAscended/mod/convert_markdown_to_json.py)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaAscended/mod/convert_markdown_to_json.py)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nMarkdown to Stationpedia JSON Converter\n\nConverts markdown guides into JSON format for the StationpediaAscended mod.\n- Game Mechanics: Uses tables and minimal nesting (flat structure)\n- Guides: Uses heavy nesting with collapsible sections\n\nUsage:\n    python convert_markdown_to_json.py [--type mechanics|guide] [--input file.md] [--output file.json]\n    python convert_markdown_to_json.py --convert-all  # Convert all to-be-implemented files\n\"\"\"\n\nimport re\nimport json\nimport os\nimport argparse\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Any, Optional, Tuple\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef slugify(text: str) -&gt; str:\n    \"\"\"Convert text to a URL-friendly slug for TOC IDs.\"\"\"\n    slug = text.lower()\n    slug = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\\s-]', '', slug)\n    slug = re.sub(r'[\\s_]+', '-', slug)\n    slug = re.sub(r'-+', '-', slug)\n    return slug.strip('-')\n\n\ndef extract_title_and_subtitle(content: str) -&gt; Tuple[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract the main title (H1) and subtitle from markdown.\"\"\"\n    title_match = re.search(r'^#\\s+(.+)$', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else \"Untitled\"\n    \n    # Look for first paragraph after title as subtitle\n    subtitle_match = re.search(r'^#\\s+.+\\n\\n(.+?)(?:\\n\\n|\\*\\*Data)', content, re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL)\n    subtitle = subtitle_match.group(1).strip() if subtitle_match else \"\"\n    \n    return title, subtitle\n\n\ndef parse_table(lines: List[str], start_idx: int) -&gt; Tuple[List[Dict[str, List[str]]], int]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a markdown table starting at the given line index.\"\"\"\n    table_rows = []\n    idx = start_idx\n    \n    while idx &lt; len(lines):\n        line = lines[idx].strip()\n        if not line.startswith('|') or line.startswith('|---') or line.startswith('| ---'):\n            # Skip separator lines\n            if '---' in line and line.startswith('|'):\n                idx += 1\n                continue\n            if not line.startswith('|'):\n                break\n        \n        # Parse table row\n        cells = [cell.strip() for cell in line.split('|')[1:-1]]\n        if cells and not all(c.startswith('---') or c.startswith('-') for c in cells):\n            table_rows.append({\"cells\": cells})\n        idx += 1\n    \n    return table_rows, idx\n\n\ndef parse_list_items(lines: List[str], start_idx: int, prefix: str = '-') -&gt; Tuple[List[str], int]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a markdown list starting at the given line index.\"\"\"\n    items = []\n    idx = start_idx\n    \n    while idx &lt; len(lines):\n        line = lines[idx].strip()\n        if line.startswith(f'{prefix} ') or line.startswith('* '):\n            # Remove the prefix and convert markdown bold to HTML\n            item_text = re.sub(r'^[-*]\\s+', '', line)\n            item_text = re.sub(r'\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*', r'&lt;b&gt;\\1&lt;/b&gt;', item_text)\n            items.append(item_text)\n        elif line.startswith('  ') and items:\n            # Continuation of previous item\n            items[-1] += ' ' + line.strip()\n        elif not line:\n            # Empty line might end the list\n            if idx + 1 &lt; len(lines) and not lines[idx + 1].strip().startswith(('-', '*', ' ')):\n                break\n        else:\n            break\n        idx += 1\n    \n    return items, idx\n\n\ndef parse_numbered_list(lines: List[str], start_idx: int) -&gt; Tuple[List[str], int]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a numbered/ordered list starting at the given line index.\"\"\"\n    items = []\n    idx = start_idx\n    \n    while idx &lt; len(lines):\n        line = lines[idx].strip()\n        match = re.match(r'^\\d+\\.\\s+(.+)$', line)\n        if match:\n            item_text = match.group(1)\n            item_text = re.sub(r'\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*', r'&lt;b&gt;\\1&lt;/b&gt;', item_text)\n            items.append(item_text)\n        elif line.startswith('  ') and items:\n            # Continuation of previous item\n            items[-1] += ' ' + line.strip()\n        elif not line:\n            if idx + 1 &lt; len(lines) and not re.match(r'^\\d+\\.', lines[idx + 1].strip()):\n                break\n        else:\n            break\n        idx += 1\n    \n    return items, idx\n\n\ndef convert_markdown_formatting(text: str) -&gt; str:\n    \"\"\"Convert markdown formatting to TextMeshPro/HTML tags.\"\"\"\n    # Bold\n    text = re.sub(r'\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*', r'&lt;b&gt;\\1&lt;/b&gt;', text)\n    # Italic\n    text = re.sub(r'\\*(.+?)\\*', r'&lt;i&gt;\\1&lt;/i&gt;', text)\n    # Code (backticks)\n    text = re.sub(r'&#96;(.+?)&#96;', r'&lt;color=#88FF88&gt;\\1&lt;/color&gt;', text)\n    # Links - convert [text](url) to just text for now\n    text = re.sub(r'\\[(.+?)\\]\\(.+?\\)', r'\\1', text)\n    return text\n\n\ndef parse_section_content(lines: List[str], start_idx: int, end_idx: int) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Parse content within a section (between headers).\"\"\"\n    content = {\n        \"description\": \"\",\n        \"items\": [],\n        \"steps\": [],\n        \"table\": []\n    }\n    \n    description_lines = []\n    idx = start_idx\n    \n    while idx &lt; end_idx:\n        line = lines[idx]\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        \n        # Skip empty lines at start\n        if not stripped and not description_lines:\n            idx += 1\n            continue\n        \n        # Table\n        if stripped.startswith('|') and not stripped.startswith('|---'):\n            table, idx = parse_table(lines, idx)\n            if table:\n                content[\"table\"] = table\n            continue\n        \n        # Numbered list (steps)\n        if re.match(r'^\\d+\\.\\s+', stripped):\n            steps, idx = parse_numbered_list(lines, idx)\n            content[\"steps\"] = steps\n            continue\n        \n        # Bullet list\n        if stripped.startswith('- ') or stripped.startswith('* '):\n            items, idx = parse_list_items(lines, idx)\n            content[\"items\"].extend(items)\n            continue\n        \n        # Code block - skip\n        if stripped.startswith('&#96;&#96;&#96;'):\n            idx += 1\n            while idx &lt; end_idx and not lines[idx].strip().startswith('&#96;&#96;&#96;'):\n                idx += 1\n            idx += 1\n            continue\n        \n        # Regular text goes to description\n        if stripped:\n            description_lines.append(convert_markdown_formatting(stripped))\n        elif description_lines and description_lines[-1]:\n            description_lines.append(\"\")  # Paragraph break\n        \n        idx += 1\n    \n    content[\"description\"] = \"\\n\".join(description_lines).strip()\n    \n    # Clean up empty fields\n    if not content[\"items\"]:\n        del content[\"items\"]\n    if not content[\"steps\"]:\n        del content[\"steps\"]\n    if not content[\"table\"]:\n        del content[\"table\"]\n    \n    return content\n\n\ndef find_sections(content: str) -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Find all sections (H2, H3, H4) in the markdown.\"\"\"\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    sections = []\n    \n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        if line.startswith('## '):\n            sections.append({\n                \"level\": 2,\n                \"title\": line[3:].strip(),\n                \"line_idx\": i\n            })\n        elif line.startswith('### '):\n            sections.append({\n                \"level\": 3,\n                \"title\": line[4:].strip(),\n                \"line_idx\": i\n            })\n        elif line.startswith('#### '):\n            sections.append({\n                \"level\": 4,\n                \"title\": line[5:].strip(),\n                \"line_idx\": i\n            })\n    \n    return sections\n\n\ndef convert_to_guide_format(content: str, guide_key: str) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Convert markdown to heavily nested guide format with collapsible sections.\"\"\"\n    title, subtitle = extract_title_and_subtitle(content)\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    sections = find_sections(content)\n    \n    guide = {\n        \"guideKey\": guide_key,\n        \"displayName\": title,\n        \"pageDescription\": subtitle,\n        \"generateToc\": True,\n        \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\n        \"buttonColor\": \"orange\",\n        \"sortOrder\": 100,\n        \"OperationalDetails\": []\n    }\n    \n    # Build hierarchical structure\n    def build_section(section_info: Dict, section_idx: int, all_sections: List[Dict], lines: List[str]) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Build a section with its children.\"\"\"\n        start_line = section_info[\"line_idx\"] + 1\n        \n        # Find end of this section (next section of same or higher level, or end)\n        end_line = len(lines)\n        children_sections = []\n        \n        for i in range(section_idx + 1, len(all_sections)):\n            next_section = all_sections[i]\n            if next_section[\"level\"] &lt;= section_info[\"level\"]:\n                end_line = next_section[\"line_idx\"]\n                break\n            elif next_section[\"level\"] == section_info[\"level\"] + 1:\n                children_sections.append((next_section, i))\n        \n        # Find where children start\n        if children_sections:\n            content_end = children_sections[0][0][\"line_idx\"]\n        else:\n            content_end = end_line\n        \n        # Parse content for this section\n        section_content = parse_section_content(lines, start_line, content_end)\n        \n        result = {\n            \"title\": section_info[\"title\"],\n            \"tocId\": slugify(section_info[\"title\"]),\n            \"collapsible\": True\n        }\n        \n        if section_content.get(\"description\"):\n            result[\"description\"] = section_content[\"description\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"items\"):\n            result[\"items\"] = section_content[\"items\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"steps\"):\n            result[\"steps\"] = section_content[\"steps\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"table\"):\n            result[\"table\"] = section_content[\"table\"]\n        \n        # Process children\n        if children_sections:\n            result[\"children\"] = []\n            for child_info, child_idx in children_sections:\n                # Check if this child belongs to us (before next sibling)\n                child_result = build_section(child_info, child_idx, all_sections, lines)\n                result[\"children\"].append(child_result)\n        \n        return result\n    \n    # Process top-level sections (H2)\n    top_level_sections = [(s, i) for i, s in enumerate(sections) if s[\"level\"] == 2]\n    \n    for section_info, section_idx in top_level_sections:\n        section = build_section(section_info, section_idx, sections, lines)\n        guide[\"OperationalDetails\"].append(section)\n    \n    return guide\n\n\ndef convert_to_mechanics_format(content: str, guide_key: str) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Convert markdown to flat game mechanics format with tables and minimal nesting.\"\"\"\n    title, subtitle = extract_title_and_subtitle(content)\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    sections = find_sections(content)\n    \n    mechanic = {\n        \"guideKey\": guide_key,\n        \"displayName\": title,\n        \"pageDescription\": subtitle,\n        \"generateToc\": True,\n        \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\n        \"buttonColor\": \"blue\",  # Different color for mechanics\n        \"sortOrder\": 50,\n        \"flatStructure\": True,  # Indicates flat/table-focused structure\n        \"OperationalDetails\": []\n    }\n    \n    # For mechanics, we flatten H2 and H3 to be at the same level\n    # and emphasize tables over nested lists\n    \n    def build_flat_section(section_info: Dict, section_idx: int, all_sections: List[Dict], lines: List[str]) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Build a flattened section for mechanics pages.\"\"\"\n        start_line = section_info[\"line_idx\"] + 1\n        \n        # Find end of this section\n        end_line = len(lines)\n        for i in range(section_idx + 1, len(all_sections)):\n            next_section = all_sections[i]\n            if next_section[\"level\"] &lt;= section_info[\"level\"]:\n                end_line = next_section[\"line_idx\"]\n                break\n        \n        # Parse content\n        section_content = parse_section_content(lines, start_line, end_line)\n        \n        result = {\n            \"title\": section_info[\"title\"],\n            \"tocId\": slugify(section_info[\"title\"]),\n            \"collapsible\": True\n        }\n        \n        if section_content.get(\"description\"):\n            result[\"description\"] = section_content[\"description\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"items\"):\n            result[\"items\"] = section_content[\"items\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"steps\"):\n            result[\"steps\"] = section_content[\"steps\"]\n        if section_content.get(\"table\"):\n            result[\"table\"] = section_content[\"table\"]\n        \n        return result\n    \n    # Process all H2 sections, but flatten H3 into separate top-level entries\n    for i, section in enumerate(sections):\n        if section[\"level\"] == 2:\n            result = build_flat_section(section, i, sections, lines)\n            mechanic[\"OperationalDetails\"].append(result)\n        elif section[\"level\"] == 3:\n            # For H3 under H2, also add as separate (flattened) entry\n            # Check if parent H2 exists\n            parent_h2 = None\n            for j in range(i - 1, -1, -1):\n                if sections[j][\"level\"] == 2:\n                    parent_h2 = sections[j]\n                    break\n            \n            result = build_flat_section(section, i, sections, lines)\n            # Optionally prefix with parent title for context\n            if parent_h2:\n                result[\"title\"] = f\"{section['title']}\"\n            mechanic[\"OperationalDetails\"].append(result)\n    \n    return mechanic\n\n\ndef generate_guide_key(filename: str, is_mechanic: bool = False) -&gt; str:\n    \"\"\"Generate a guideKey from the filename.\"\"\"\n    base = Path(filename).stem\n    # Convert kebab-case to PascalCase\n    parts = base.replace('_', '-').split('-')\n    pascal = ''.join(word.capitalize() for word in parts)\n    \n    if is_mechanic:\n        return f\"Mechanic{pascal}\"\n    return f\"Guide{pascal}\"\n\n\ndef process_markdown_file(filepath: str, output_type: str = \"auto\") -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Process a single markdown file and return the JSON structure.\"\"\"\n    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n    \n    filename = Path(filepath).stem\n    \n    # Auto-detect type based on path or content\n    if output_type == \"auto\":\n        if \"Game Mechanics\" in filepath or \"game-mechanic\" in filepath.lower():\n            output_type = \"mechanics\"\n        else:\n            output_type = \"guide\"\n    \n    is_mechanic = output_type == \"mechanics\"\n    guide_key = generate_guide_key(filename, is_mechanic)\n    \n    if is_mechanic:\n        return convert_to_mechanics_format(content, guide_key)\n    else:\n        return convert_to_guide_format(content, guide_key)\n\n\ndef convert_all_to_be_implemented(base_path: str) -&gt; Tuple[List[Dict], List[Dict]]:\n    \"\"\"Convert all markdown files in the To Be Implemented folder.\"\"\"\n    guides_path = Path(base_path) / \"Guides\" / \"To Be Implemented\" / \"Guides\"\n    mechanics_path = Path(base_path) / \"Guides\" / \"To Be Implemented\" / \"Game Mechanics\"\n    \n    guides = []\n    mechanics = []\n    \n    # Process guides\n    if guides_path.exists():\n        for md_file in guides_path.glob(\"*.md\"):\n            print(f\"Processing guide: {md_file.name}\")\n            result = process_markdown_file(str(md_file), \"guide\")\n            guides.append(result)\n    \n    # Process game mechanics\n    if mechanics_path.exists():\n        for md_file in mechanics_path.glob(\"*.md\"):\n            print(f\"Processing mechanic: {md_file.name}\")\n            result = process_markdown_file(str(md_file), \"mechanics\")\n            mechanics.append(result)\n    \n    return guides, mechanics\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Convert markdown to Stationpedia JSON format\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--type\", choices=[\"mechanics\", \"guide\", \"auto\"], default=\"auto\",\n                       help=\"Output type: mechanics (flat/tables) or guide (nested)\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--input\", \"-i\", help=\"Input markdown file\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output\", \"-o\", help=\"Output JSON file\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--convert-all\", action=\"store_true\",\n                       help=\"Convert all files in To Be Implemented folder\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--base-path\", default=\".\",\n                       help=\"Base path to the mod folder\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--preview\", action=\"store_true\",\n                       help=\"Print JSON to stdout instead of writing to file\")\n    \n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    \n    if args.convert_all:\n        guides, mechanics = convert_all_to_be_implemented(args.base_path)\n        \n        output = {\n            \"guides\": guides,\n            \"mechanics\": mechanics\n        }\n        \n        if args.preview:\n            print(json.dumps(output, indent=2))\n        else:\n            output_file = args.output or \"converted_entries.json\"\n            with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n                json.dumps(output, f, indent=2)\n            print(f\"\\nConverted {len(guides)} guides and {len(mechanics)} mechanics\")\n            print(f\"Output written to: {output_file}\")\n            print(\"\\nTo add these to descriptions.json:\")\n            print(\"1. Open the output file\")\n            print(\"2. Add 'guides' entries to the 'guides' array in descriptions.json\")\n            print(\"3. Add 'mechanics' entries to the 'guides' array (with 'gameMechanic' button)\")\n    \n    elif args.input:\n        result = process_markdown_file(args.input, args.type)\n        \n        if args.preview:\n            print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))\n        else:\n            output_file = args.output or f\"{Path(args.input).stem}.json\"\n            with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n                json.dump(result, f, indent=2)\n            print(f\"Output written to: {output_file}\")\n    \n    else:\n        parser.print_help()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
  "terminalCommandOutput": {
    "text": "\u001b[36mchanics\\smelting-complete-guide.md\" \u001b[90m--type \u001b[37mmechanics \u001b[90m--preview\r\n\u001b[0m{\r\n  \"guideKey\": \"MechanicSmeltingCompleteGuide\",\r\n  \"displayName\": \"Complete Smelting Guide for Stationeers\",\r\n  \"pageDescription\": \"---\",\r\n  \"generateToc\": true,\r\n  \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\r\n  \"buttonColor\": \"blue\",\r\n  \"sortOrder\": 50,\r\n  \"flatStructure\": true,\r\n  \"OperationalDetails\": [\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Section 1: Introduction &amp; Furnace Types\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"section-1-introduction-furnace-types\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"Smelting is the gateway to advanced construction in Stationeers. Without it, you're stuck with basic materials. Understanding which furnace to use is your first decision.\\n\\n### The Three Furnace Types\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Arc Furnace&lt;/b&gt;\\n&lt;b&gt;Furnace (Standard)&lt;/b&gt;\\n&lt;b&gt;Advanced Furnace&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\n### Key Concept\\n\\nThe furnace combines whatever's inside when temperature and pressure conditions are met. &lt;b&gt;YOU&lt;/b&gt; must load the correct ratios before igniting. Ratios must be exact - 3:1 means exactly 3:1, not approximately.\\n\\n### Safety Warning\\n\\nAll furnaces radiate heat into surrounding atmosphere. Your base WILL heat up. Plan for ventilation or isolate your smelting area.\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"Electric-powered, no combustion needed\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Ores only&lt;/b&gt; - cannot make alloys\",\r\n        \"Perfect for bulk ingot production\",\r\n        \"Best for: Iron, Gold, Copper, Silicon, Nickel, Cobalt, Lead ingots\",\r\n        \"Use when: You need lots of basic ingots and have power to spare\",\r\n        \"Combustion-based, requires fuel gas (H2 + O2 from ice)\",\r\n        \"Makes steel and basic alloys\",\r\n        \"This is what most players build first\",\r\n        \"Makes: Steel, Solder, Electrum, Invar, Constantan\",\r\n        \"Use when: You need alloys for construction and electronics\",\r\n        \"Required for super-alloys\",\r\n        \"Higher pressure tolerance (max 60 MPa before explosion)\",\r\n        \"Makes: Stellite, Inconel, Hastelloy, Astroloy, Waspaloy\",\r\n        \"Use when: Building rockets or endgame equipment\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"The Three Furnace Types\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"the-three-furnace-types\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;b&gt;Arc Furnace&lt;/b&gt;\\n&lt;b&gt;Furnace (Standard)&lt;/b&gt;\\n&lt;b&gt;Advanced Furnace&lt;/b&gt;\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"Electric-powered, no combustion needed\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Ores only&lt;/b&gt; - cannot make alloys\",\r\n        \"Perfect for bulk ingot production\",\r\n        \"Best for: Iron, Gold, Copper, Silicon, Nickel, Cobalt, Lead ingots\",\r\n        \"Use when: You need lots of basic ingots and have power to spare\",\r\n        \"Combustion-based, requires fuel gas (H2 + O2 from ice)\",\r\n        \"Makes steel and basic alloys\",\r\n        \"This is what most players build first\",\r\n        \"Makes: Steel, Solder, Electrum, Invar, Constantan\",\r\n        \"Use when: You need alloys for construction and electronics\",\r\n        \"Required for super-alloys\",\r\n        \"Higher pressure tolerance (max 60 MPa before explosion)\",\r\n        \"Makes: Stellite, Inconel, Hastelloy, Astroloy, Waspaloy\",\r\n        \"Use when: Building rockets or endgame equipment\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Key Concept\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"key-concept\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"The furnace combines whatever's inside when temperature and pressure conditions are met. &lt;b&gt;YOU&lt;/b&gt; must load the correct ratios before igniting. Ratios must be exact - 3:1 means exactly 3:1, not approximately.\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Safety Warning\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"safety-warning\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"All furnaces radiate heat into surrounding atmosphere. Your base WILL heat up. Plan for ventilation or isolate your smelting area.\\n\\n---\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Section 2: Your First Smelt - Steel\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"section-2-your-first-smelt-steel\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"Steel is every player's first alloy and the most common failure point. Master this before attempting anything else.\\n\\n### Recipe (Verified)\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Conditions:&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\n### What You Need\\n\\n### The Process\\n\\n### Why It Works\\n\\n### Common Failures\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"Temperature: 900 K - 100 kK (wide range, easy to hit)\",\r\n        \"Pressure: 1 - 100 MPa (wide range, easy to hit)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Furnace (Standard)&lt;/b&gt; - not Arc Furnace!\",\r\n        \"Iron and Coal in exact 3:1 ratio\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;2 Volatiles Ice + 1 Oxite Ice&lt;/b&gt; (provides H2 fuel + O2 oxidizer)\",\r\n        \"Volatiles ice releases H2 (hydrogen) - the fuel\",\r\n        \"Oxite ice releases O2 (oxygen) - the oxidizer\",\r\n        \"Combustion creates heat AND pressure simultaneously\",\r\n        \"Steel's wide acceptable ranges (900K+, 1 MPa+) make it forgiving\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"Load ores FIRST - Iron and Coal in 3:1 ratio (e.g., 75 Iron + 25 Coal)\",\r\n        \"Load ice - 2 Volatiles, 1 Oxite\",\r\n        \"Close the furnace\",\r\n        \"Activate (click igniter)\",\r\n        \"Wait - furnace will reach ~1050K and ~6.2 MPa\",\r\n        \"When \\\"Output: Steel\\\" shows and temp/pressure stabilize, open and collect\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Problem\",\r\n            \"Cause\",\r\n            \"Solution\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Nothing happened\",\r\n            \"Didn't ignite\",\r\n            \"Check the activation port, use welder or igniter\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Got iron ingots instead\",\r\n            \"Pressure or temp too low\",\r\n            \"Add more ice\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Furnace exploded\",\r\n            \"Too much ice\",\r\n            \"Standard furnace maxes at ~90 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Ores disappeared, no output\",\r\n            \"Wrong ratio\",\r\n            \"Must be exactly 3:1 Iron:Coal\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Recipe (Verified)\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"recipe-verified\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;b&gt;Conditions:&lt;/b&gt;\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"Temperature: 900 K - 100 kK (wide range, easy to hit)\",\r\n        \"Pressure: 1 - 100 MPa (wide range, easy to hit)\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Component\",\r\n            \"Ratio\",\r\n            \"Example Quantities\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Iron Ore\",\r\n            \"3\",\r\n            \"12, 15, 75\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Coal Ore\",\r\n            \"1\",\r\n            \"4, 5, 25\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"What You Need\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"what-you-need\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Furnace (Standard)&lt;/b&gt; - not Arc Furnace!\",\r\n        \"Iron and Coal in exact 3:1 ratio\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;2 Volatiles Ice + 1 Oxite Ice&lt;/b&gt; (provides H2 fuel + O2 oxidizer)\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"The Process\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"the-process\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"Load ores FIRST - Iron and Coal in 3:1 ratio (e.g., 75 Iron + 25 Coal)\",\r\n        \"Load ice - 2 Volatiles, 1 Oxite\",\r\n        \"Close the furnace\",\r\n        \"Activate (click igniter)\",\r\n        \"Wait - furnace will reach ~1050K and ~6.2 MPa\",\r\n        \"When \\\"Output: Steel\\\" shows and temp/pressure stabilize, open and collect\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Why It Works\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"why-it-works\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"Volatiles ice releases H2 (hydrogen) - the fuel\",\r\n        \"Oxite ice releases O2 (oxygen) - the oxidizer\",\r\n        \"Combustion creates heat AND pressure simultaneously\",\r\n        \"Steel's wide acceptable ranges (900K+, 1 MPa+) make it forgiving\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Common Failures\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"common-failures\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Problem\",\r\n            \"Cause\",\r\n            \"Solution\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Nothing happened\",\r\n            \"Didn't ignite\",\r\n            \"Check the activation port, use welder or igniter\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Got iron ingots instead\",\r\n            \"Pressure or temp too low\",\r\n            \"Add more ice\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Furnace exploded\",\r\n            \"Too much ice\",\r\n            \"Standard furnace maxes at ~90 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Ores disappeared, no output\",\r\n            \"Wrong ratio\",\r\n            \"Must be exactly 3:1 Iron:Coal\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Section 3: Common Alloys\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"section-3-common-alloys\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"All use the Standard Furnace. &lt;b&gt;Critical:&lt;/b&gt; These have NARROW operating windows unlike steel.\\n\\n### Solder\\n&lt;i&gt;For electronics, circuits, cables&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Temperature has a MAX of 550K. Too hot = failure. This is the only alloy where you might need to COOL the furnace or use less ice.\\n\\n### Electrum\\n&lt;i&gt;For advanced electronics, some sensors&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Pressure has a narrow window (0.8-2.4 MPa). Too much ice will overshoot. Use minimal ice - 1 Volatiles + 1 Oxite or less.\\n\\n### Invar\\n&lt;i&gt;For precision instruments, advanced frames&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; BOTH windows are narrow. Temperature must be 1.2-1.5 kK AND pressure must be 18-20 MPa. This is one of the hardest basic alloys. You need significant ice but precise amounts.\\n\\n### Constantan\\n&lt;i&gt;For heating elements, thermocouples&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; High minimum pressure (20 MPa) but wide ranges otherwise. Easier than Invar once you get enough pressure.\\n\\n### Alloy Difficulty Ranking\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Steel&lt;/b&gt; - Easiest (wide ranges)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Solder&lt;/b&gt; - Easy but watch max temp\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Constantan&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (need high pressure)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Electrum&lt;/b&gt; - Tricky (narrow pressure window)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Invar&lt;/b&gt; - Hardest basic alloy (both windows narrow)\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"1,000 K\",\r\n            \"10 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"20 MPa\",\r\n            \"100 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Solder\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"solder\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;For electronics, circuits, cables&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Temperature has a MAX of 550K. Too hot = failure. This is the only alloy where you might need to COOL the furnace or use less ice.\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"350 K\",\r\n            \"550 K\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"1 MPa\",\r\n            \"100 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Electrum\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"electrum\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;For advanced electronics, some sensors&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Pressure has a narrow window (0.8-2.4 MPa). Too much ice will overshoot. Use minimal ice - 1 Volatiles + 1 Oxite or less.\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"600 K\",\r\n            \"100 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"0.8 MPa\",\r\n            \"2.4 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Invar\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"invar\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;For precision instruments, advanced frames&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; BOTH windows are narrow. Temperature must be 1.2-1.5 kK AND pressure must be 18-20 MPa. This is one of the hardest basic alloys. You need significant ice but precise amounts.\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"1,200 K\",\r\n            \"1,500 K\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"18 MPa\",\r\n            \"20 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Constantan\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"constantan\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;For heating elements, thermocouples&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; High minimum pressure (20 MPa) but wide ranges otherwise. Easier than Invar once you get enough pressure.\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"1,000 K\",\r\n            \"10 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"20 MPa\",\r\n            \"100 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Alloy Difficulty Ranking\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"alloy-difficulty-ranking\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"---\",\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Steel&lt;/b&gt; - Easiest (wide ranges)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Solder&lt;/b&gt; - Easy but watch max temp\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Constantan&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (need high pressure)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Electrum&lt;/b&gt; - Tricky (narrow pressure window)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Invar&lt;/b&gt; - Hardest basic alloy (both windows narrow)\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Section 4: Super-Alloys\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"section-4-super-alloys\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"Super-alloys require the &lt;b&gt;Advanced Furnace&lt;/b&gt; and have tight operating windows. These recipes use &lt;b&gt;common ores plus Steel ingots&lt;/b&gt; - not exotic ores.\\n\\n### Critical Rules for ALL Super-Alloys\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Stellite\\n&lt;i&gt;High-wear applications, cutting tools&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Most forgiving super-alloy. Wide temperature range. Good first attempt.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Inconel\\n&lt;i&gt;Heat shields, rocket components&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; That 0.5 MPa pressure window is brutal. Requires precise pressure control. Monitor closely and vent small amounts to dial in.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Hastelloy\\n&lt;i&gt;Corrosion-resistant components&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Temperature window is only 50K (950-1000K). Pressure window is reasonable at 5 MPa range.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Astroloy\\n&lt;i&gt;Aerospace, high-stress applications&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Requires Steel Ingots (smelt steel first). 10 MPa pressure window is workable. Wide temp range.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Waspaloy\\n&lt;i&gt;Ultimate high-temp applications&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Highest pressure requirement (50+ MPa) but widest pressure window. Temperature is counterintuitively LOW - don't overheat!\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Super-Alloy Difficulty Ranking\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Stellite&lt;/b&gt; - Easiest (wide ranges, common ores)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Astroloy&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (needs Steel Ingots, reasonable windows)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Waspaloy&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (high pressure but wide windows)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Hastelloy&lt;/b&gt; - Hard (tight 50K temp window)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Inconel&lt;/b&gt; - Hardest (0.5 MPa pressure window!)\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"400 K\",\r\n            \"800 K\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"50 MPa\",\r\n            \"100 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Critical Rules for ALL Super-Alloys\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"critical-rules-for-all-super-alloys\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"---\",\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Use Advanced Furnace only&lt;/b&gt; (60 MPa max capacity)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Recipes use ingots AND ores&lt;/b&gt; - some require Steel Ingots, not ores\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Monitor pressure/temp constantly&lt;/b&gt; - windows can be very narrow\",\r\n        \"As of 2021, recipes return only &lt;b&gt;1 ingot per batch&lt;/b&gt;\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Stellite\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"stellite\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;High-wear applications, cutting tools&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Most forgiving super-alloy. Wide temperature range. Good first attempt.\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"1,800 K\",\r\n            \"100 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"10 MPa\",\r\n            \"20 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Inconel\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"inconel\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;Heat shields, rocket components&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; That 0.5 MPa pressure window is brutal. Requires precise pressure control. Monitor closely and vent small amounts to dial in.\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"600 K\",\r\n            \"100 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"23.5 MPa\",\r\n            \"24 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Hastelloy\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"hastelloy\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;Corrosion-resistant components&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Warning:&lt;/b&gt; Temperature window is only 50K (950-1000K). Pressure window is reasonable at 5 MPa range.\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"950 K\",\r\n            \"1,000 K\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"25 MPa\",\r\n            \"30 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Astroloy\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"astroloy\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;Aerospace, high-stress applications&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Requires Steel Ingots (smelt steel first). 10 MPa pressure window is workable. Wide temp range.\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"1,000 K\",\r\n            \"100 kK\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"30 MPa\",\r\n            \"40 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Waspaloy\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"waspaloy\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;i&gt;Ultimate high-temp applications&lt;/i&gt;\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Notes:&lt;/b&gt; Highest pressure requirement (50+ MPa) but widest pressure window. Temperature is counterintuitively LOW - don't overheat!\\n\\n---\",\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Condition\",\r\n            \"Min\",\r\n            \"Max\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Temperature\",\r\n            \"400 K\",\r\n            \"800 K\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Pressure\",\r\n            \"50 MPa\",\r\n            \"100 MPa\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Super-Alloy Difficulty Ranking\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"super-alloy-difficulty-ranking\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"---\",\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Stellite&lt;/b&gt; - Easiest (wide ranges, common ores)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Astroloy&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (needs Steel Ingots, reasonable windows)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Waspaloy&lt;/b&gt; - Medium (high pressure but wide windows)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Hastelloy&lt;/b&gt; - Hard (tight 50K temp window)\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Inconel&lt;/b&gt; - Hardest (0.5 MPa pressure window!)\"\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Section 5: Safety &amp; Troubleshooting\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"section-5-safety-troubleshooting\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"Furnaces will kill you. They'll explode, cook your base, or waste hours of ore mining. Here's how to survive.\\n\\n### Explosion Prevention\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Prevention Rules:&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\n### Heat Management\\n\\nFurnaces radiate heat into surrounding atmosphere. A 1000K furnace WILL cook your base and kill you.\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Solutions:&lt;/b&gt;\\n\\n### Troubleshooting Quick Reference\\n\\n### Emergency Procedures\\n\\n&lt;b&gt;Overheating base:&lt;/b&gt;\\n&lt;b&gt;Pressure climbing dangerously:&lt;/b&gt;\\n---\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Never overload ice&lt;/b&gt; - 3 Volatiles + 2 Oxite is usually the safe max\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Watch the pressure gauge&lt;/b&gt; during smelting\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Vent early&lt;/b&gt; if pressure climbing dangerously\",\r\n        \"Vent atmosphere to space immediately\",\r\n        \"Seal furnace room from living area\",\r\n        \"Open furnace to vent (loses heat too)\",\r\n        \"Or use attached pipe to bleed off gas gradually\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"steps\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Isolate it&lt;/b&gt; - Put furnace in separate room with airlock, vent to space after each smelt\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Vacuum smelting&lt;/b&gt; - No atmosphere = no heat transfer to base\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Active cooling&lt;/b&gt; - Wall coolers + radiators if you want it indoors\",\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Distance&lt;/b&gt; - Further from living space = more time to react\"\r\n      ],\r\n      \"table\": [\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Problem\",\r\n            \"Cause\",\r\n            \"Fix\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Nothing happens when ignited\",\r\n            \"No fuel gas or no O2\",\r\n            \"Check ice loaded, try more Oxite\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Got base ingots instead of alloy\",\r\n            \"Temp or pressure outside range\",\r\n            \"Check BOTH min AND max values\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Wrong alloy came out\",\r\n            \"Wrong ore ratio\",\r\n            \"Ratios must be exact\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Ores vanished, nothing output\",\r\n            \"Invalid ratio\",\r\n            \"Check recipe again\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Furnace exploded\",\r\n            \"Overpressure\",\r\n            \"Use less ice\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Base is heating up\",\r\n            \"Normal furnace behavior\",\r\n            \"Isolate or vacuum smelt\"\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          \"cells\": [\r\n            \"Alloy won't form despite correct values\",\r\n            \"Outside MAX temp/pressure\",\r\n            \"Some alloys have narrow windows - check maximums\"\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"title\": \"Explosion Prevention\",\r\n      \"tocId\": \"explosion-prevention\",\r\n      \"collapsible\": true,\r\n      \"description\": \"&lt;b&gt;Prevention Rules:&lt;/b&gt;\",\r\n      \"items\": [\r\n        \"&lt;b&gt;Never overload ice&lt;/b&gt; - {
```
```json
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Clean up title - remove \"Game Mechanic:\" prefix if present\r\n    clean_title = title.replace(\"Game Mechanic:\", \"\").strip()\r\n    \r\n    mechanic = {\r\n        \"guideKey\": guide_key,\r\n        \"displayName\": clean_title,\r\n        \"pageDescription\": subtitle if subtitle != \"---\" else \"\",\r\n        \"generateToc\": True,\r\n        \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\r\n        \"buttonColor\": \"blue\",  # Different color for mechanics\r\n        \"sortOrder\": 50,\r\n        \"flatStructure\": True,  # Indicates flat/table-focused structure\r\n        \"OperationalDetails\": []\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    # For mechanics, we process ONLY H3 sections directly (skip H2 headers)\r\n    # This gives us a flat list of topics with their tables\r\n    \r\n    def build_flat_section(section_info: Dict, section_idx: int, all_sections: List[Dict], lines: List[str]) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Build a flattened section for mechanics pages.\"\"\"\r\n        start_line = section_info[\"line_idx\"] + 1\r\n        \r\n        # Find end of this section (next section of same or higher level)\r\n        end_line = len(lines)\r\n        for i in range(section_idx + 1, len(all_sections)):\r\n            next_section = all_sections[i]\r\n            if next_section[\"level\"] &lt;= section_info[\"level\"]:\r\n                end_line = next_section[\"line_idx\"]\r\n                break\r\n        \r\n        # Parse content\r\n        section_content = parse_section_content(lines, start_line, end_line)\r\n        \r\n        result = {\r\n            \"title\": section_info[\"title\"],\r\n            \"tocId\": slugify(section_info[\"title\"]),\r\n            \"collapsible\": True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        # Clean up description - remove \"---\" markers\r\n        desc = section_content.get(\"description\", \"\")\r\n        desc = desc.replace(\"\\n\\n---\", \"\").replace(\"---\\n\\n\", \"\").replace(\"---\", \"\").strip()\r\n        \r\n        if desc:\r\n            result[\"description\"] = desc\r\n        if section_content.get(\"items\"):\r\n            result[\"items\"] = section_content[\"items\"]\r\n        if section_content.get(\"steps\"):\r\n            result[\"steps\"] = section_content[\"steps\"]\r\n        if section_content.get(\"table\"):\r\n            result[\"table\"] = section_content[\"table\"]\r\n        \r\n        return result\r\n    \r\n    # Process only H3 sections for flat mechanics format\r\n    for i, section in enumerate(sections):\r\n        if section[\"level\"] == 3:\r\n            result = build_flat_section(section, i, sections, lines)\r\n            # Skip empty sections\r\n            if result.get(\"description\") or result.get(\"items\") or result.get(\"steps\") or result.get(\"table\"):\r\n    \n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "# Clean up title - remove \"Game Mechanic:\" prefix if present\r\n    clean_title = title.replace(\"Game Mechanic:\", \"\").strip()\r\n    \r\n    mechanic = {\r\n        \"guideKey\": guide_key,\r\n        \"displayName\": clean_title,\r\n        \"pageDescription\": subtitle if subtitle != \"---\" else \"\",\r\n        \"generateToc\": True,\r\n        \"tocTitle\": \"Contents\",\r\n        \"buttonColor\": \"blue\",  # Different color for mechanics\r\n        \"sortOrder\": 50,\r\n        \"flatStructure\": True,  # Indicates flat/table-focused structure\r\n        \"OperationalDetails\": []\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    # For mechanics, we process ONLY H3 sections directly (skip H2 headers)\r\n    # This gives us a flat list of topics with their tables\r\n    \r\n    def build_flat_section(section_info: Dict, section_idx: int, all_sections: List[Dict], lines: List[str]) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Build a flattened section for mechanics pages.\"\"\"\r\n        start_line = section_info[\"line_idx\"] + 1\r\n        \r\n        # Find end of this section (next section of same or higher level)\r\n        end_line = len(lines)\r\n        for i in range(section_idx + 1, len(all_sections)):\r\n            next_section = all_sections[i]\r\n            if next_section[\"level\"] &lt;= section_info[\"level\"]:\r\n                end_line = next_section[\"line_idx\"]\r\n                break\r\n        \r\n        # Parse content\r\n        section_content = parse_section_content(lines, start_line, end_line)\r\n        \r\n        result = {\r\n            \"title\": section_info[\"title\"],\r\n            \"tocId\": slugify(section_info[\"title\"]),\r\n            \"collapsible\": True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        # Clean up description - remove \"---\" markers\r\n        desc = section_content.get(\"description\", \"\")\r\n        desc = desc.replace(\"\\n\\n---\", \"\").replace(\"---\\n\\n\", \"\").replace(\"---\", \"\").strip()\r\n        \r\n        if desc:\r\n            result[\"description\"] = desc\r\n        if section_content.get(\"items\"):\r\n            result[\"items\"] = section_content[\"items\"]\r\n        if section_content.get(\"steps\"):\r\n            result[\"steps\"] = section_content[\"steps\"]\r\n        if section_content.get(\"table\"):\r\n            result[\"table\"] = section_content[\"table\"]\r\n        \r\n        return result\r\n    \r\n    # Process only H3 sections for flat mechanics format\r\n    for i, section in enumerate(sections):\r\n        if section[\"level\"] == 3:\r\n            result = build_flat_section(section, i, sections, lines)\r\n            # Skip empty sections\r\n            if result.get(\"description\") or result.get(\"items\") or result.get(\"steps\") or result.get(\"table\"):\r\n    ",
```

---

The user wants me to create a tool that will convert markdown files into JSON format for their Stationpedia mod. They have two types of content:

1. **Game Mechanics** - in "to be implemented game mechanics" folder → should become game mechanic pages with tables and less nesting
2. **Guides** - in "to be implemented guides" folder → should become heavily nested guides similar to existing ones

Let me first explore the folder structure to understand:
1. Where are these markdown files located?
2. What's the format of existing JSON entries in descriptions.json?
3. What's the structure of existing guides vs game mechanic pages?

Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and understanding the data formats.Let me explore the folder structure and understand the existing formats first: Let me look at examples of existing guides in JSON format and the markdown files to understand the conversion: Now let me see an existing guide in JSON format to understand the exact structure: Now let me also check the schema and see how game mechanics pages might differ: Now let me read other guide markdown files to understand both formats better: Now I have a good understanding of the formats. Let me create the converter tool as a Python script: Now let me test it on one of the markdown files: The converter is working. The mechanics format is flattening sections properly and extracting tables. There are some duplicated sections because the flat mode is including both H2 and H3 sections. Let me improve the script to better handle the hierarchy and also test the guide format: Now let me test with converting all files and also test a guide format: Small bug - should be `json.dump` not `json.dumps`. Let me fix that: Let me check the output file to verify it looks correct: Excellent! The converter is working! Let me check the mechanics format: The mechanics format is working well - flat list of H3 sections with tables preserved. The converter is ready! Let me provide a summary and usage instructions: I've created the markdown to JSON converter tool. Here's a summary:

## Converter Tool Created: `convert_markdown_to_json.py`

### Usage:

**Convert all files in To Be Implemented:**
```bash
python convert_markdown_to_json.py --convert-all --base-path "." --output converted_entries.json
```

**Convert a single file:**
```bash
# For guides (heavily nested):
python convert_markdown_to_json.py --input "file.md" --type guide --output guide.json

# For game mechanics (flat with tables):
python convert_markdown_to_json.py --input "file.md" --type mechanics --output mechanic.json

# Preview without writing file:
python convert_markdown_to_json.py --input "file.md" --preview
```

### What it does:

**Game Mechanics Format:**
- Extracts only H3 sections (flat list, no nesting)
- Preserves tables
- Used for informational pages under "Game Mechanics" button
- `buttonColor: "blue"`, `flatStructure: true`

**Guides Format:**
- Full nested hierarchy (H2 → H3 → H4 as children)
- Collapsible sections
- Used for tutorial/how-to content
- `buttonColor: "orange"`, with `children` arrays

### Output file: `converted_entries.json`

The output contains:
- `"guides"`: 3 guides (AIMEE, LARRE, Stirling Engine)
- `"mechanics"`: 2 mechanics (Smelting, Species Survival)

To add to the game, copy entries from the output file into your `descriptions.json` "guides" array.

```json
  "isConfirmed": {
```

```json
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Merge converted entries into descriptions.json\"\"\"\nimport json\n\n# Read converted entries\nwith open('converted_entries.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    converted = json.load(f)\n\n# Read existing descriptions\nwith open('descriptions.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    descriptions = json.load(f)\n\n# Add the new guides\nif 'guides' in converted:\n    descriptions['guides'].extend(converted['guides'])\n    print(f\"Added {len(converted['guides'])} new guides\")\n\n# Add the new mechanics\nif 'mechanics' in converted:\n    descriptions['mechanics'].extend(converted['mechanics'])\n    print(f\"Added {len(converted['mechanics'])} new mechanics\")\n\n# Write the merged file\nwith open('descriptions.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    json.dump(descriptions, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n\nprint('Successfully merged entries into descriptions.json')\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

---

The user is saying they don't see the game mechanic pages in the mechanics tab of the editor. Let me look at how the editor loads and displays mechanics vs guides to understand why the mechanics aren't showing up.

The converted entries were added to descriptions.json with:
- 3 guides in the "guides" array
- 2 mechanics in the "mechanics" array

But the editor might not be loading the mechanics array properly, or there might be an issue with how the mechanics are being displayed.

Let me check the editor code to see how it handles mechanics vs guides.Based on the search results, I can see that the editor has a "mechanics" tab and the mod uses a `GameMechanicsRegistry` to track mechanics pages. The mechanics in descriptions.json are stored in a separate "mechanics" array.

Let me look at the editor code to see how it handles the mechanics tab - specifically how it loads and displays mechanics entries.Let me look at the editor code to see how it loads mechanics: The ContentTree looks correct - it's using `workspace.mechanics` to display mechanics. Let me check how the workspace is loaded and if the mechanics array is being populated correctly: The loading looks correct. Let me check the fileService to see how it reads the mechanics from the JSON file: I see the issue! The `loadWorkspace` function is looking for mechanics in a `game-mechanics` folder, not from the `descriptions.json` file's `mechanics` array. Let me look more closely at how guides are loaded vs mechanics: Found the issue! The `loadWorkspace` function loads guides from `descriptions.json` but loads mechanics from a separate `game-mechanics` folder. It doesn't load mechanics from the `mechanics` array in `descriptions.json`. I need to add that: Now I need to also update the save function to include mechanics in the `descriptions.json` file. Let me check how it saves: Good news - the save function already handles mechanics! It reads `descriptions.mechanics` and saves it back. The issue was just in the loading. Now let me rebuild the editor to test: The TypeScript and Vite build succeeded but electron-builder failed due to a symbolic link permissions issue. Let me use the dev build instead which should work: The editor is running. The fix has been applied - now when you reload the workspace, the mechanics should load from `descriptions.json`. 

To summarize the fix: The `loadWorkspace` function in fileService.ts was loading guides from `descriptions.json` but only loading mechanics from a separate `game-mechanics` folder. I added code to also load mechanics from the `mechanics` array in `descriptions.json`, similar to how guides are loaded.

**To see your mechanics:**
1. Close and reopen the editor (or reload the workspace)
2. Click on the "Mechanics" tab 
3. You should now see the 2 mechanics entries (Smelting Complete Guide, Species Survival Guide)

Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaAscended/mod/src/Data/JsonMechanicsLoader.cs)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaAscended/mod/src/Data/JsonMechanicsLoader.cs)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing Assets.Scripts;\nusing Assets.Scripts.UI;\nusing UnityEngine;\n\nnamespace StationpediaAscended.Data\n{\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\n    /// Loads and manages Game Mechanics pages from descriptions.json.\n    /// These pages appear under the \"Game Mechanics\" button in Stationpedia.\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n    public static class JsonMechanicsLoader\n    {\n        private static Dictionary&lt;string, GuideDescription&gt; _loadedMechanics = new Dictionary&lt;string, GuideDescription&gt;();\n        private static bool _mechanicsRegistered = false;\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Load mechanics from the parsed DescriptionsRoot data\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static void LoadMechanics(DescriptionsRoot data)\n        {\n            _loadedMechanics.Clear();\n\n            if (data?.mechanics == null || data.mechanics.Count == 0)\n            {\n                ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[Stationpedia Ascended] No game mechanics found in descriptions.json\");\n                return;\n            }\n\n            foreach (var mechanic in data.mechanics)\n            {\n                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(mechanic.guideKey))\n                {\n                    ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Skipping mechanic with no guideKey\");\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                _loadedMechanics[mechanic.guideKey] = mechanic;\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Loaded mechanic: {mechanic.displayName ?? mechanic.guideKey}\");\n            }\n\n            ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Loaded {_loadedMechanics.Count} game mechanics from JSON\");\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Register all loaded mechanics as Stationpedia pages and with GameMechanicsRegistry\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static void RegisterMechanicsPages()\n        {\n            if (_mechanicsRegistered) return;\n\n            foreach (var kvp in _loadedMechanics)\n            {\n                try\n                {\n                    var mechanic = kvp.Value;\n                    \n                    // Create a StationpediaPage for this mechanic\n                    var page = new StationpediaPage\n                    {\n                        Key = mechanic.guideKey,\n                        Title = mechanic.displayName ?? mechanic.guideKey\n                    };\n\n                    // Set introductory text from pageDescription\n                    page.Text = mechanic.pageDescription ?? \"\";\n\n                    // Register the page with Stationpedia\n                    Stationpedia.Register(page, false);\n\n                    // Also register with GameMechanicsRegistry so it shows up in the listing\n                    GameMechanicsRegistry.RegisterPage(mechanic.guideKey);\n\n                    ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Registered mechanic page: {mechanic.guideKey}\");\n                }\n                catch (Exception ex)\n                {\n                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError($\"Error registering mechanic {kvp.Key}: {ex.Message}\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            _mechanicsRegistered = true;\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Get all loaded mechanics sorted by sortOrder\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static List&lt;GuideDescription&gt; GetAllMechanics()\n        {\n            return _loadedMechanics.Values\n                .OrderBy(m =&gt; m.sortOrder)\n                .ThenBy(m =&gt; m.displayName ?? m.guideKey)\n                .ToList();\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Get a specific mechanic by key\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static GuideDescription GetMechanic(string mechanicKey)\n        {\n            if (_loadedMechanics.TryGetValue(mechanicKey, out var mechanic))\n            {\n                return mechanic;\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Check if a mechanic exists\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static bool HasMechanic(string mechanicKey)\n        {\n            return _loadedMechanics.ContainsKey(mechanicKey);\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Convert a mechanic (GuideDescription) to DeviceDescriptions for rendering\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static DeviceDescriptions ToDeviceDescriptions(GuideDescription mechanic)\n        {\n            if (mechanic == null) return null;\n\n            return new DeviceDescriptions\n            {\n                deviceKey = mechanic.guideKey,\n                displayName = mechanic.displayName,\n                pageDescription = mechanic.pageDescription,\n                pageDescriptionPrepend = mechanic.pageDescriptionPrepend,\n                pageDescriptionAppend = mechanic.pageDescriptionAppend,\n                pageImage = mechanic.pageImage,\n                operationalDetails = mechanic.operationalDetails,\n                operationalDetailsTitleColor = mechanic.operationalDetailsTitleColor,\n                generateToc = mechanic.generateToc,\n                tocTitle = mechanic.tocTitle,\n                tocFlat = mechanic.tocFlat,\n                operationalDetailsBackgroundColor = mechanic.operationalDetailsBackgroundColor\n            };\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Clear all loaded mechanics (for hot-reload)\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static void Clear()\n        {\n            _loadedMechanics.Clear();\n            _mechanicsRegistered = false;\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Parse button color from mechanic settings\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static Color GetButtonColor(GuideDescription mechanic)\n        {\n            if (mechanic == null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(mechanic.buttonColor))\n            {\n                // Default to blue for game mechanics\n                return new Color(0f, 0.54f, 0.90f, 1f); // #008AE6\n            }\n\n            var colorStr = mechanic.buttonColor.ToLower();\n            \n            if (colorStr == \"blue\")\n            {\n                return new Color(0f, 0.54f, 0.90f, 1f); // #008AE6\n            }\n            else if (colorStr == \"orange\")\n            {\n                return new Color(1f, 0.42f, 0.09f, 1f); // #FF6A18\n            }\n            else if (colorStr == \"green\")\n            {\n                return new Color(0.27f, 0.68f, 0.51f, 1f); // #44AD83\n            }\n            else if (colorStr.StartsWith(\"#\"))\n            {\n                // Try to parse hex color\n                if (ColorUtility.TryParseHtmlString(colorStr, out var parsedColor))\n                {\n                    return parsedColor;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Default to blue\n            return new Color(0f, 0.54f, 0.90f, 1f);\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\na JSON-defined game mechanic page - sections appear directly without wrapper\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void RenderJsonMechanicPage(UniversalPage page, Transform contentTransform, string mechanicKey)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;\r\n                if (stationpedia == null) return;\r\n\r\n                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;\r\n                if (categoryPrefab == null) return;\r\n\r\n                // Get the mechanic data\r\n                var mechanic = Data.JsonMechanicsLoader.GetMechanic(mechanicKey);\r\n                if (mechanic == null)\r\n                {\r\n                    ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Mechanic not found: {mechanicKey}\");\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n\r\n                // Convert to DeviceDescriptions for rendering\r\n                var mechanicData = Data.JsonMechanicsLoader.ToDeviceDescriptions(mechanic);\r\n                if (mechanicData?.operationalDetails == null || mechanicData.operationalDetails.Count == 0)\r\n                {\r\n                    // If no sections, just show the page description\r\n                    ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Mechanic {mechanicKey} has no sections\");\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n\r\n                // Render sections directly without \"Operational Details\" wrapper\r\n                RenderGuideSections(page, contentTransform, mechanicData, mechanic.generateToc, mechanic.tocTitle);\r\n                \r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] page, Transform contentTransform, string mechanicKey)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;\r\n                if (stationpedia == null) return;\r\n\r\n                var categoryPrefab = stationpedia.CategoryPrefab;\r\n                if (categoryPrefab == null) return;\r\n\r\n                // Get the mechanic data\r\n mechanicData = Data.JsonMechanicsLoader.ToDeviceDescriptions(mechanic);\r\n                if (mechanicData?.operationalDetails == null || mechanicData.operationalDetails.Count == 0)\r\n The user is saying that the game mechanics guides aren't showing up in the game when they press the Game Mechanics button. This is different from the editor issue - this is about the actual mod in the game.
```

Let me look at how the Game Mechanics pages are registered and displayed in the mod. The issue might be that the mechanics entries need to be registered with the GameMechanicsRegistry for them to show up on the Game Mechanics page.

Let me search for how pages are registered and how the Game Mechanics listing is populated.Now let me see how pages are registered. The mechanics pages need to be registered with `GameMechanicsRegistry.RegisterPage()`. Let me check where guide pages are registered to see the pattern: Let me search for how the guides are being loaded and registered to see the pattern: Now I see the pattern. There's a `JsonGuideLoader` that loads guides, but I need to check if there's a similar loader for mechanics, or if mechanics also need to be registered with `GameMechanicsRegistry`. Let me check how the mechanics in `descriptions.json` should be processed: Let me look at where guides from `descriptions.json` are loaded and see if there's code to also load mechanics: Let me search for where the mechanics loading should happen and the DescriptionsRoot model: I see! The `DescriptionsRoot` model has `guides` but no `mechanics` property! The mechanics aren't being loaded from the JSON because there's no model property for them. I need to:

1. Add a `mechanics` property to `DescriptionsRoot`
2. Load the mechanics similar to how guides are loaded
3. Register them with `GameMechanicsRegistry`

Let me make these changes: Now let me add a loader for mechanics and update the loading code. First, let me create a mechanic loader similar to the guide loader: Now let me update the main mod file to also load mechanics. Let me find the places where guides are loaded and add mechanics loading: There are multiple places where this code appears. Let me search for all instances and update them with more context: Now I need to find where the guide pages are registered and add mechanics registration. Let me search for where `RegisterGuidePages` is called: I see - `RegisterGuidePages` is called during the guide listing. Similarly, I need to make sure `RegisterMechanicsPages` is called. Let me look at the `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` function that shows the mechanics list: I see the issue - `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` is using `GameMechanicsRegistry.GameMechanicsPages` but this list is populated by `RegisterMechanicsPages()`. The mechanics need to be registered before they can be shown. Let me update `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` to use the `JsonMechanicsLoader` and register mechanics: Now I need to make sure the mechanic pages render correctly when visited. Let me check if there's a handler for rendering mechanic pages similar to guide pages: I see - there's code to check for guide pages and render them with `RenderJsonGuidePage`. I need to add similar handling for mechanics pages. Let me add the check for mechanics: Now I need to create the `RenderJsonMechanicPage` method. Let me find where `RenderJsonGuidePage` is defined and add a similar method: I can add a similar `RenderJsonMechanicPage` method right after `RenderJsonGuidePage`. Let me add it: Now let me build the mod to test: The mod built successfully and the DLL + `descriptions.json` have been copied to the game folder. 

Now the game mechanics should work. To summarize what I fixed:

1. **Added `mechanics` property to `DescriptionsRoot`** in Models.cs - The JSON model now knows about the `mechanics` array

2. **Created `JsonMechanicsLoader.cs`** - A new loader class that handles loading and registering mechanics pages, similar to `JsonGuideLoader`

3. **Updated `StationpediaAscended.cs`** - Added calls to load mechanics from `descriptions.json` in both loading locations

4. **Updated `PopulateGameMechanicsContents`** in Patches.cs - Now uses `JsonMechanicsLoader` to get and display mechanics pages

5. **Added `RenderJsonMechanicPage`** method - Handles rendering mechanic pages when they're visited

6. **Added check for mechanic pages** in `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` - Detects when a mechanic page is being viewed and renders it correctly

The game mechanics pages should now appear when you click the "Game Mechanics" button in the Stationpedia, and clicking on them should render the content properly.

Let me check git status: We can restore it from git: Good, now let me verify the original content is back and then fix the merge script to properly append instead of overwrite: The original guides are back. Now let me fix the merge script to properly append entries without overwriting existing ones: Now let's run the fixed merge script: Now we have all 9 guides (6 original + 3 new) and 2 mechanics. - DaylightSensorGuide
- PowerSystemsGuide
- AirlockGuide

- MechanicSpeciesSurvivalGuide

style: React.CSSProperties; isDragging: boolean }) =&gt; React.ReactNode;\r\n}\r\n\r\nconst SortableSection: React.FC&lt;SortableSectionProps&gt; = ({ id, children }) =&gt; {\r\n  const { attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition, isDragging } =\r\n    useSortable({ id });\r\n\r\n  const style: React.CSSProperties = {\r\n    transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform),\r\n    transition,\r\n    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    &lt;div ref={setNodeRef} style={style}&gt;\r\n      {children({ dragHandleProps: { ...attributes, ...listeners }, style, isDragging })}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
```json
  "textEditContent": "  showDragHandle?: boolean; // Show drag handle for reordering\r\n  dragHandleProps?: any; // Props from useSortable for drag handle\r\n}&gt; = ({ title, isExpanded, onToggle, onTitleClick, onAddTable, onAddHeader, onAddSubsection, onAddImage, onAddVideo, showDelete, onDelete, isSelected, showDragHandle, dragHandleProps }) =&gt; {\r\n  const [isDropdownOpen, setIsDropdownOpen] = useState(false);\r\n  const dropdownRef = useRef&lt;HTMLDivElement&gt;(null);\r\n\r\n  // Close dropdown when clicking outside\r\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\r\n    const handleClickOutside = (event: MouseEvent) =&gt; {\r\n      if (dropdownRef.current &amp;&amp; !dropdownRef.current.contains(event.target as Node)) {\r\n        setIsDropdownOpen(false);\r\n      }\r\n    };\r\n    if (isDropdownOpen) {\r\n      document.addEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);\r\n    }\r\n    return () =&gt; document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);\r\n  }, [isDropdownOpen]);\r\n\r\n  const hasAnyAddAction = onAddTable || onAddHeader || onAddSubsection || onAddImage || onAddVideo;\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    &lt;div className={&#96;flex items-center justify-between py-2 px-3 bg-stationpedia-surface/80 rounded-t border ${isSelected ? 'border-stationpedia-accent bg-stationpedia-accent/20' : 'border-stationpedia-border'}&#96;}&gt;\r\n      &lt;div className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"&gt;\r\n        {/* Drag Handle - 6 dots (only shown when showDragHandle=true) */}\r\n        {showDragHandle &amp;&amp; dragHandleProps &amp;&amp; (\r\n          &lt;div\r\n            {...dragHandleProps}\r\n            className=\"flex-shrink-0 cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing text-gray-500 hover:text-stationpedia-accent p-1\"\r\n            title=\"Drag to reorder section\"\r\n          &gt;\r\n            &lt;DragHandleIcon className=\"w-4 h-4\" /&gt;\r\n          &lt;/div&gt;\r\n        )}\r\n        &lt;button\r\n          onClick={onToggle}\r\n          className=\"text-xs text-gray-400 hover:text-white p-1\"\r\n          title={isExpanded ? 'Collapse' : 'Expand'}\r\n        &gt;\r\n          {isExpanded ? '▼' : '▶'}\r\n        &lt;/button&gt;\r\n        {onTitleClick ?
```
